#====================================================================================================
# START - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================

# THIS SECTION CONTAINS CRITICAL TESTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR BOTH AGENTS
# BOTH MAIN_AGENT AND TESTING_AGENT MUST PRESERVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK

# Communication Protocol:
# If the `testing_agent` is available, main agent should delegate all testing tasks to it.
#
# You have access to a file called `test_result.md`. This file contains the complete testing state
# and history, and is the primary means of communication between main and the testing agent.
#
# Main and testing agents must follow this exact format to maintain testing data. 
# The testing data must be entered in yaml format Below is the data structure:
# 
## user_problem_statement: {problem_statement}
## backend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.py"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## frontend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.js"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## metadata:
##   created_by: "main_agent"
##   version: "1.0"
##   test_sequence: 0
##   run_ui: false
##
## test_plan:
##   current_focus:
##     - "Task name 1"
##     - "Task name 2"
##   stuck_tasks:
##     - "Task name with persistent issues"
##   test_all: false
##   test_priority: "high_first"  # or "sequential" or "stuck_first"
##
## agent_communication:
##     -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##     -message: "Communication message between agents"

# Protocol Guidelines for Main agent
#
# 1. Update Test Result File Before Testing:
#    - Main agent must always update the `test_result.md` file before calling the testing agent
#    - Add implementation details to the status_history
#    - Set `needs_retesting` to true for tasks that need testing
#    - Update the `test_plan` section to guide testing priorities
#    - Add a message to `agent_communication` explaining what you've done
#
# 2. Incorporate User Feedback:
#    - When a user provides feedback that something is or isn't working, add this information to the relevant task's status_history
#    - Update the working status based on user feedback
#    - If a user reports an issue with a task that was marked as working, increment the stuck_count
#    - Whenever user reports issue in the app, if we have testing agent and task_result.md file so find the appropriate task for that and append in status_history of that task to contain the user concern and problem as well 
#
# 3. Track Stuck Tasks:
#    - Monitor which tasks have high stuck_count values or where you are fixing same issue again and again, analyze that when you read task_result.md
#    - For persistent issues, use websearch tool to find solutions
#    - Pay special attention to tasks in the stuck_tasks list
#    - When you fix an issue with a stuck task, don't reset the stuck_count until the testing agent confirms it's working
#
# 4. Provide Context to Testing Agent:
#    - When calling the testing agent, provide clear instructions about:
#      - Which tasks need testing (reference the test_plan)
#      - Any authentication details or configuration needed
#      - Specific test scenarios to focus on
#      - Any known issues or edge cases to verify
#
# 5. Call the testing agent with specific instructions referring to test_result.md
#
# IMPORTANT: Main agent must ALWAYS update test_result.md BEFORE calling the testing agent, as it relies on this file to understand what to test next.

#====================================================================================================
# END - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================



#====================================================================================================
# Testing Data - Main Agent and testing sub agent both should log testing data below this section
#====================================================================================================

user_problem_statement: "Test the 4 newly-added/modified backend features: 200-level XP system (/api/levels), un-tick (uncomplete) restored, custom task XP cap = 20 (defaults unrestricted), anonymous mode via X-Anonymous-Id header."

backend:
  - task: "Chat preferences (per-friend colors, mute, soft-block) + integrations"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/chat_preferences.py + /app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 67/67 assertions in /app/chat_preferences_test.py (run 2026-05-16 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Two fresh gmail.com users (Alice Chatcolor + Bob Mutey) plus admin login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com. Backend log shows '[chat_preferences] routes attached' on each reload. Verified end-to-end:\n\n  ✅ STEP 1-2 Bulk empty default: A GET /chat/preferences → 200 {preferences:[]} (empty list).\n  ✅ STEP 3 Single-friend defaults: A GET /chat/preferences/<B> → 200 with sent_bubble_color='#00E1FF', sent_text_color='#0A0A0F', received_bubble_color='#1A1A24', received_text_color='#E6E6F0', muted=false, blocked=false, updated_at=null (all 7 default fields verbatim).\n  ✅ STEP 4 Upsert subset: A POST /chat/preferences/<B> {sent_bubble_color:'#FF6FB5', muted:true} → 200 with response.sent_bubble_color='#FF6FB5', muted=true, blocked=false, updated_at non-null ISO (parses via datetime.fromisoformat). _serialize round-trip correctly preserves all field types.\n  ✅ STEP 5 Bulk after upsert: A GET /chat/preferences → 200 with preferences:[{friend_id==B, sent_bubble_color='#FF6FB5', muted:true, blocked:false, ...}]. Length=1.\n  ✅ STEP 6 Hex validation: A POST /chat/preferences/<B> {sent_bubble_color:'#ZZZZZZ'} → 422 (Pydantic regex HEX_RE rejects).\n  ✅ STEP 7 /mute convenience route: A POST /chat/preferences/<B>/mute {value:false} → 200, muted:false, sent_bubble_color preserved '#FF6FB5' (confirms partial-upsert semantics — only the muted field is touched, color from step 4 stays).\n  ✅ STEP 8 /block convenience route: A POST /chat/preferences/<B>/block {value:true} → 200, blocked:true, muted:false.\n  ✅ STEP 9 Friendship setup: B POST /friends/request {user_id:A} → 200; A POST /friends/accept {user_id:B} → 200 status='friends'.\n  ✅ STEP 10 messages_send with blocker: B POST /messages/send {to_user_id:A, refined_text:'hello A', original_text:'hello A'} → 200; message.id is uuid; message.refined_text=='hello A' (inserted into db.messages). Push suppression path (suppress_push=True) is exercised internally — endpoint still 200s as required.\n  ✅ STEP 11 unread-summary blocker suppression: A GET /messages/unread-summary → 200; unread_by_friend does NOT contain B's user_id (filtered out by _chat_blocked_for(user_id) at server.py:6139-6145). total_unread=0 (excludes B-only count).\n  ✅ STEP 12 messages_threads blocker row: A GET /messages/threads → 200; B's thread row present with unread_count==0 AND blocked:true (the new field at server.py:6090).\n  ✅ STEP 13 Soft-block history readable: A GET /messages/thread/<B> → 200 with B's 'hello A' message visible in the messages list (soft block — history is readable, just the badge/push is suppressed).\n  ✅ STEP 14 Unblock restores badge: A POST /chat/preferences/<B>/block {value:false} → 200 blocked:false; A GET /messages/unread-summary → 200 with B's user_id NOW in unread_by_friend with count >=1.\n  ✅ STEP 15 Mute DOES NOT suppress badge: A POST /chat/preferences/<B>/mute {value:true} → 200 muted:true; A GET /messages/unread-summary STILL contains B's user_id (mute suppresses push only, not badge). Cleanup: mute:false → 200.\n  ✅ STEP 16 Self friend_id rejection: A POST /chat/preferences/<A.user_id> {muted:true} → 400 with detail EXACTLY 'Invalid friend id.' (per _upsert_impl + _get_impl at chat_preferences.py:150,157).\n  ✅ STEP 17 Regression no-prefs flow: fresh users C+D registered, befriended, D POST /messages/send → C → 200; C GET /messages/unread-summary → D's user_id present in unread_by_friend (no pref row → no suppression, default behaviour preserved).\n  ✅ STEP 18 Admin push exception: admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com) login OK; A POST /chat/preferences/<admin.user_id>/block {value:true} → 200 blocked:true; admin POST /messages/send {to_user_id:A, refined_text:'admin ping'} → 200; message.id present (inserted into db.messages). A GET /messages/thread/<admin.user_id> → 200 with 'admin ping' visible. Confirms server.py:6023 short-circuit: 'if not sender_is_admin and _chat_pref_for_pair is not None' — admin pushes are NEVER suppressed, per spec.\n\n  Marked working:true. All 67 assertions pass across all 6 chat_preferences endpoints (bulk GET, single GET, upsert POST, /mute, /block, plus the 2 helpers get_pref_for_pair & list_blocked_for via integrations in messages_send/messages_threads/messages_unread_summary). HEX_RE regex correctly rejects '#ZZZZZZ' with 422; '#FF6FB5' (6-char) accepted; defaults persisted correctly. Soft-block semantics work as designed: blocker can still read history + reply, just no push + no badge. Admin override path verified end-to-end. No regressions in /messages/send, /messages/threads, /messages/thread/{friend_id}, /messages/unread-summary for users without any chat_preferences rows."

  - task: "Goal time-lock (first tick) + Goal XP add/refund on tick/untick + goal_xp chart segment"
    implemented: true
    working: false
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 1
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW (2026-05-12): Goal cadence locking + green chart segment.\\n(1) GOAL_CYCLE_LOCKOUT now uses 30 days for months (was 29). 7 days for weeks (unchanged). Daily / Yearly unchanged.\\n(2) `_is_goal_locked(goal)` extended: when the goal has NEVER been ticked AND its unit is 'weeks' or 'months', the function returns (True, created_at + lockout) if `now < created_at + lockout`. This fixes the bug where a newly-created Monthly goal with target_value=1 could be auto-completed on the same day. The `update_goal_progress` endpoint already calls `_is_goal_locked` before allowing an increment so the 429 'cycle_locked' response now also fires for the FIRST tick on weekly/monthly goals within the lockout window.\\n(3) `update_goal_progress` completion side-effects unchanged in terms of XP add (still `+xp_reward` to profile.total_xp + `_log_xp_to_charts`), BUT now also (a) persists `xp_awarded_on_complete` on the goal doc so a future un-tick refunds the EXACT same amount (scoped by goal_id per user spec, even if xp_reward was edited later), (b) records `last_completed_at` for analytics, (c) tags the chart row with `goal_id` and `kind='goal_complete'`.\\n(4) Un-tick (going from completed:true → completed:false) now: (a) refunds `xp_awarded_on_complete` (fallback to xp_reward for legacy goals), (b) deletes the matching task_logs row(s) scoped by (user_id, goal_id, kind='goal_complete') so the green segment vanishes from the chart for that day, (c) clears `xp_awarded_on_complete`.\\n(5) `_log_xp_to_charts` gained optional keyword args goal_id + kind. When source='goal_complete', caller passes both so the chart rows are tagged for filtering / un-tick deletion.\\n(6) NEW chart key `goal_xp` on /stats/weekly + /stats/monthly + /admin/players/{id}/charts: sums task_logs.{kind:'goal_complete', user_id} grouped by date. The earned-xp sum (xp key) now EXCLUDES `goal_complete` rows to avoid double-counting (goal XP is surfaced via the green segment instead). The `tasks` counter also excludes kind in ('penalty','goal_complete') so the per-day quest count stays accurate."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "MOSTLY PASS but ONE CRITICAL BUG (2026-05-12, /app/goal_lock_test.py + /app/goal_admin_bypass_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Admin login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com + fresh gmail.com user A registered + admin-bypass round-trip on a fresh daily goal with xp_reward=150.\\n\\n  ❌ BUG #1 (REAL, blocks the spec): POST /api/goals response is MISSING the `is_locked` and `next_tick_available_at` fields. Verified twice: weekly goal create response keys = ['id','user_id','title','description','focus_area','target_value','current_value','unit','xp_reward','completed','created_at','completed_at']. Spec explicitly says: 'POST /api/goals body {...} → 200 with goal that has is_locked=true and next_tick_available_at = created_at + 30 days.' The cause: `create_goal` at server.py L1923-1961 does NOT call `_enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` before `return goal`. Only `list_goals` (L1917) and `update_goal_progress` (L2102) enrich. ONE-LINE FIX: insert `goal = _enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` right before the `return goal` at L1961. Without this fix, the frontend can't detect a freshly-created Monthly/Weekly goal is locked from the create response alone — it has to re-fetch via GET /goals.\\n\\n  ✅ EVERYTHING ELSE WORKS:\\n   (1) MONTHLY first-tick lock: POST /api/goals (target=1, unit='months', xp_reward=200) → 200. Subsequent POST /goals/{id}/progress {cv:1} → **429** with detail={error:'cycle_locked', next_tick_available_at:'<ISO>', unit:'months', message:...}. profile.total_xp UNCHANGED after the failed tick. THE TARGET=1 BUG FIX VERIFIED — without the fix it would 200 and award 200 XP.\\n   (2) WEEKLY first-tick lock: same shape, unit='weeks', 7-day lockout from created_at. 429 with detail.unit=='weeks'. total_xp UNCHANGED.\\n   (3) DAILY goal target=1 still works (no lockout): POST /goals (xp_reward=150 as admin to bypass _clamp_goal_xp) → 200. POST /progress {cv:1} → 200 with response.completed=true, response.awarded_xp=150 EXACTLY. profile.total_xp delta = +180 (= +150 completion + +30 daily-step XP from the existing per-step daily-goal feature — NOT a regression). xp_awarded_on_complete=150 persisted on the goal doc (verified via GET /goals).\\n   (4) UN-TICK refund (scoped by goal_id): POST /progress {cv:0} on the completed daily goal → 200, response.completed=false, response.refunded_xp=150 EXACTLY. profile.total_xp delta = -180 (refunds the +150 completion AND the +30 step XP). Full round-trip net total_xp == starting value, no drift. task_logs goal_complete row deletion VERIFIED INDIRECTLY: after un-tick, /stats/weekly today's goal_xp drops back to 0 (the goal_complete row was deleted from task_logs). Re-creating + re-completing a 2nd daily goal pushes goal_xp back to 30 (for non-admin) or 150 (for admin) on the same-day row.\\n   (5) STATS goal_xp KEY: /stats/weekly returns 7 days, /stats/monthly returns 30 days. EVERY day has `goal_xp` as an int. Today's row reflects the actual sum from task_logs.kind='goal_complete'. The `xp` key correctly EXCLUDES goal_complete rows (verified: today.xp != today.goal_xp). Tested with daily goal xp_reward=150 (admin) → today.goal_xp=150; same for monthly endpoint.\\n   (6) ADMIN CHARTS: GET /admin/players/{userA}/charts → 200 with weekly.days length=7, monthly.days length=30, EVERY entry carries goal_xp:int. Admin charts today.goal_xp matches user's /stats/weekly today.goal_xp.\\n   (7) REGRESSIONS: /api/penalties/pending → 200, /api/penalties/history?limit=50 → 200, /api/stats/by-area → 200. Pre-existing daily goals keep working with no lockout.\\n\\n  📊 TEST RUN STATS: goal_lock_test.py = 37 PASS / 14 FAIL but most FAILs are due to the existing non-admin _clamp_goal_xp clamp (xp_reward=150 → clamped to 30 for non-admin daily goals — this is the documented GOAL_XP_CAPS['days']=30 cap from server.py:953 and NOT a regression). goal_admin_bypass_test.py = 13 PASS / 4 FAIL where 2 of 4 FAILs are the missing-enrichment bug and 2 are the +30 daily-step XP layered on top (existing behavior — see test_result.md prior status_history about `_source='goal_step'`).\\n\\n  ⚠️ TEST-SPEC INFOMATION (NOT a bug, just clarification for main agent):\\n   (a) The review spec says 'Daily/Yearly... POST progress {cv:1} → awarded_xp=150. profile.total_xp +=150'. For NON-ADMIN users this is incorrect because _clamp_goal_xp clamps days→30. For ADMIN users it's correct (no clamp). The 4-test admin-bypass run verifies exact 150 awarded_xp + 150 refunded_xp.\\n   (b) Daily goals award an EXTRA +30 XP per-step on the increment edge (from a prior PR — see _source='goal_step' rows). So a daily goal with xp_reward=150 + target=1 deltas total_xp by +180 (150 completion + 30 step) on tick and -180 on un-tick. Net round-trip is 0. Both response fields awarded_xp/refunded_xp report ONLY the 150 completion amount (the step delta is not surfaced in the same response field but IS reflected in profile.total_xp). Symmetric, no drift.\\n\\n  REQUIRED ACTION: Add `goal = _enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` (or equivalent inline `is_locked`/`next_tick_available_at` computation) at server.py:1960 right before `return goal` in `create_goal`. Marking working:false until that single-line fix lands."

  - task: "XP Penalty Subtraction (Creator-only) + Live public profile fields"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/penalties.py + /app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW (2026-05-12): (1) New module /app/backend/penalties.py exposes 5 endpoints — POST /api/admin/players/{player_id}/penalty (creator only; body {amount:int>=1, note:str<=2000}), GET /api/penalties/pending (caller-only, unread), POST /api/penalties/{penalty_id}/acknowledge (caller-only), GET /api/penalties/history?limit=50 (caller-only, full), GET /api/admin/players/{player_id}/penalties (creator-only, any player's history). Wired via app.include_router(sub) at end of server.py. \\n(2) Apply-penalty side-effects: inserts into NEW collection xp_penalties (_id uuid, creator_id, player_id, amount, note, date='YYYY-MM-DD', created_at ISO, acknowledged_at null). Subtracts amount from profile.total_xp (XP allowed to go NEGATIVE per user spec). Recomputes profile.level via level_from_xp(max(0, new_total_xp)) so the level cap on display is 0, but XP can still be negative under the hood. Mirrors a negative-XP row into task_logs ({xp_awarded:-amount, kind:'penalty', penalty_id}) so charts can surface the BLACK overlay. Fires Expo push to all push_tokens for that player (best-effort).\\n(3) /api/stats/weekly + /api/stats/monthly + /api/admin/players/{id}/charts now return penalty_xp per day (positive int representing amount subtracted that day) alongside xp/gifted_xp/tasks. Also: tasks count now excludes kind=='penalty' rows so the counter stays accurate. Earned xp summing clamps negatives to 0 so the bar doesn't double-subtract.\\n(4) _serialize_player gained a live_extras kwarg AND derives level from total_xp on every read; /friends/profile/{other_id} now passes a freshly-computed bundle (tasks_completed via task_logs.count_documents({xp_awarded:{$gt:0}}), active_goals_count+total_goals_count+goals_completed via goals.count_documents). Also adds active_goals_count, total_goals_count, joined_at fields to the public payload so the profile card is truly LIVE on every open."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 87/87 assertions in /app/penalty_subtraction_test.py (run 2026-05-12 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Admin login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com OK. Two fresh gmail.com regular users (Alice Morgan + Ben Parker) registered. Verified end-to-end:\n\n  ✅ POST /api/admin/players/{id}/penalty:\n    (a) Non-admin (userB Bearer) → 403 EXACTLY with detail='Creator only.'\n    (b) Admin with amount=0 → 422 (Pydantic ge=1, exact error 'Input should be greater than or equal to 1')\n    (c) Admin with amount=-5 → 422 (same Pydantic validator)\n    (d) Admin with {amount:200, note:'test penalty'} → 200 with EXACT response shape {ok:true, penalty_id:'5c4063c8-…' (uuid), player_id:userA, amount:200, note:'test penalty', new_total_xp:-200 (NEGATIVE confirmed), new_level:1, created_at:'2026-05-12T09:48:32.217273+00:00' (ISO)}. penalty_id parses as valid uuid via uuid.UUID(). created_at parses via datetime.fromisoformat.\n\n  ✅ Profile total_xp deduction VERIFIED:\n    Pre-penalty GET /friends/profile/{userA} as admin → total_xp=0. Post-penalty → total_xp=-200 (decreased by exactly 200, may be negative confirmed). Also cross-verified via /admin/players/by-creation → that user's row.total_xp=-200.\n\n  ✅ Task-logs mirror row VERIFIED (indirectly via stats + tasks_completed):\n    pre-penalty tasks_completed=0, post-penalty tasks_completed=0 (UNCHANGED). This confirms /friends/profile excludes the negative-XP penalty mirror row from tasks_completed count (uses task_logs.count_documents({xp_awarded:{$gt:0}}) which filters out the -200 row). AND today's stats penalty_xp=200 confirms the xp_penalties row exists for today.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/penalties/pending (caller-only):\n    (a) Before penalty (userA Bearer) → 200 {penalties:[]} (empty list)\n    (b) After penalty → 200 with the new penalty row present, shape EXACTLY {id, creator_id (==admin_id), player_id (==userA), amount:200, note:'test penalty', created_at:ISO, date:'2026-05-12', acknowledged_at:null}\n\n  ✅ POST /api/penalties/{penalty_id}/acknowledge (caller-only):\n    (a) As userB (different user) → 404 with detail='Penalty not found.'\n    (b) As userA (receiving user) → 200 {ok:true, penalty_id:<pid>}\n    (c) Calling AGAIN as userA → 200 {ok:true, already:true} (idempotent)\n\n  ✅ GET /api/penalties/history?limit=50 (caller-only):\n    As userA → 200 with the same penalty, acknowledged_at NOW set to an ISO timestamp (parsed successfully via datetime.fromisoformat)\n\n  ✅ GET /api/admin/players/{userA}/penalties:\n    (a) As admin → 200 with the same record present\n    (b) As non-admin (userB) → 403\n\n  ✅ Stats endpoints carry new `penalty_xp`:\n    (a) GET /api/stats/weekly as userA → 200, days length=7, EACH day has all 6 keys {date, day, xp, gifted_xp, penalty_xp, tasks}. Today's row (date=='2026-05-12') has penalty_xp==200 EXACTLY.\n    (b) GET /api/stats/monthly as userA → 200, days length=30, EACH day has the 6 expected keys.\n    (c) GET /api/admin/players/{userA}/charts as admin → 200, weekly.days length=7, monthly.days length=30. BOTH weekly AND monthly entries carry penalty_xp on every entry (all 7+30 rows). Today's weekly entry has penalty_xp==200.\n\n  ✅ Live public profile freshness:\n    GET /api/friends/profile/{userA} as admin → response payload contains ALL required new fields with correct types: active_goals_count (int, =0), total_goals_count (int, =0), joined_at (str ISO, =created_at), tasks_completed (int, live from task_logs.count_documents({xp_awarded:{$gt:0}}) — verified excludes the penalty's -200 mirror row), level (int, derived via level_from_xp(max(0, total_xp))). After the penalty, total_xp=-200 reflects the deduction (negative integer confirmed).\n\n  Marked working:true. All 87 assertions pass. No regressions, no bugs found. Penalty system + live profile fields are wired correctly across all 5 new endpoints plus the 3 modified stats endpoints. Negative-XP storage works as designed (level capped at 0 for display but total_xp can go negative under the hood)."

  - task: "Admin player charts + Chat moderation softening (image/refine/admin-bypass)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py + /app/frontend/app/admin/player/[id].tsx"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 36/36 assertions in /app/admin_charts_moderation_test.py (run 2026-05-11 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Admin login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com OK. Two fresh gmail.com regular users (userA + userB) registered. Verified end-to-end:\n\n  ✅ SECTION 1 — GET /api/admin/players/{player_id}/charts:\n    (1a) Non-admin (userA Bearer) → 403 EXACTLY with detail='Admin only.'\n    (1b) Admin → admin_own_user_id → 200 with response keys EXACTLY {user_id, weekly, monthly, by_area}. user_id is str and == admin_id. weekly is {days:[…]} length 7. monthly is {days:[…]} length 30. by_area is dict. Each day has all 5 expected keys {date,day,xp,gifted_xp,tasks}. date is ISO yyyy-mm-dd (validated via date.fromisoformat). day is str (weekday for weekly e.g. 'Tue', day-of-month string for monthly e.g. '14'). xp/gifted_xp/tasks are int. by_area keys EXACTLY == FOCUS_AREAS=('social','fitness','appearance','mindset') with int ≥ 0 values (e.g. {'social':0,'fitness':40,'appearance':0,'mindset':0}).   - task: "XP Exploit Fix — Done/Undone leaderboard sync + per-task rate limit"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "XP EXPLOIT FIX (2026-05-09): Bug — _log_xp_event() previously rejected xp <= 0, so when /api/tasks/{id}/uncomplete reversed total_xp on the profile it never wrote a negative event into xp_events → the weekly leaderboard kept the inflated value. Toggling Done/Undone in a loop pumped infinite leaderboard XP. FIX: (1) Removed the xp<=0 guard in _log_xp_event so negative deltas now insert into xp_events; the leaderboard sums them, correctly cancelling the matching positive event. (2) /api/tasks/{id}/uncomplete now also calls `_log_xp_event(user_id, -awarded, tz_off)` after deleting the task_log. (3) Added a process-local sliding-window rate limiter `_check_task_toggle_rate(user_id, task_id)` — max 6 toggles per 5s per (user, task); exceed → HTTP 429 {error:'task_toggle_rate_limited', retry_after_seconds:N}. Both /complete and /uncomplete call it on entry. PLEASE TEST: (a) Done/Undone loop 3x: starting total_xp=X, complete a 50-XP task → total_xp=X+50, leaderboard weekly_xp+=50; uncomplete → total_xp=X, weekly_xp back to original; repeat 3 times → final total_xp == X (exact equality, no drift), weekly_xp identical to before. (b) Spam protection: fire 7 complete/uncomplete alternating calls within 1s → the 7th returns HTTP 429 with detail.error=='task_toggle_rate_limited' and detail.retry_after_seconds is a positive number ≤5. (c) Wait 6 seconds → next call succeeds (window cleared). (d) Idempotency: complete twice in a row (no uncomplete between) → second returns {already_completed:true} with no extra XP added. uncomplete twice → second returns {already_uncompleted:true} with no extra XP removed. (e) Regression: a clean single complete still awards XP correctly (profile.total_xp increments by xp_value*multiplier, weekly_xp increments by same, /stats/by-area & /stats/weekly still reflect the gain via task_logs). Use admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 42/42 assertions in /app/xp_exploit_test.py (run 2026-05-09 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api, admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com). NOTE: review request mentioned GET /api/leaderboard but the actual endpoint is GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (matches the row by is_self=true) — used that throughout. Created custom 50-XP mindset tasks via admin (bypasses 20-XP non-admin cap at server.py L1640). Verified end-to-end:\n\n  ✅ [4] SINGLE-COMPLETE REGRESSION — POST /tasks/{id}/complete on a fresh 50-XP mindset task → 200 with body.xp_awarded=50; profile.total_xp delta=+50 (50→55 starting from prior 5 fragments); /friends/leaderboard self.weekly_xp delta=+50; /stats/by-area.mindset delta=+50; /stats/weekly today.xp delta>=+50. All four surfaces aligned.\n\n  ✅ [1] DONE/UNDONE LOOP STAYS BALANCED (THE BUG FIX) — baseline total_xp=55, weekly_xp=0. Loop 3× {complete → uncomplete} with 1.1s spacing between toggles → all 6 toggles return 200. Final total_xp=55 EXACTLY (drift=0), final weekly_xp=0 EXACTLY (no inflation). The previously-broken behaviour (xp_events append-only with positive-only guard) would have inflated weekly_xp by +150 across the 3 completes; the negative-event fix correctly cancels every +50 with a matching -50.\n\n  ✅ [2] RATE LIMIT (HTTP 429) — fired 7 rapid alternating complete/uncomplete calls; statuses=[200,200,200,200,200,200,429]. Body: {detail:{error:'task_toggle_rate_limited', message:\"You're toggling this task too quickly. Try again in 4.3s.\", retry_after_seconds:4.3}}. retry_after_seconds=4.3 is a float ≤5 (✓). After waiting 6s, the next /complete call returned 200 (window cleared). Confirms the 5s sliding-window with max=6 toggles per (user_id, task_id) at server.py L1701-1729.\n\n  ✅ [3] IDEMPOTENCY — (a) /complete twice in a row (1.1s apart): first → 200 with xp_awarded=50, second → 200 with body={already_completed:true, task:{...}} and zero additional XP (after_first=after_second=105) and weekly_xp not double-counted (stayed at +50). (b) /uncomplete twice in a row (1.1s apart, on a non-completed task): first uncomplete legitimately returned 200 + reverted XP, second → 200 with body={already_uncompleted:true} and zero additional XP removed (no underflow). The keys 'already_completed' and 'already_uncompleted' match the spec verbatim.\n\n  ✅ [5] REVERSE EVENT EXISTENCE — complete + uncomplete pair with 1.1s spacing → profile total_xp net 0 AND leaderboard weekly_xp net 0. The leaderboard sum being net-zero is direct proof that a -50 event was appended into xp_events and the leaderboard aggregator correctly summed +50 + (-50) = 0.\n\n  ✅ [6] CONCURRENCY BACK-TO-BACK — complete then uncomplete with NO sleep (313ms total elapsed) → both 200, total_xp unchanged, weekly_xp unchanged. Confirms the 6-per-5s threshold accommodates legitimate fast toggles.\n\n  Marked working:true. The fix is solid: negative-event logging, idempotent already_*, rate limiting, and net-zero leaderboard math all work as specified. No regressions, no XP drift, no bugs found."

No errors in backend logs related to silence-window check; the new skipped_silence counter at server.py L5023+L5042 executes cleanly (the broadcast log line `[match.invite.broadcast] match=… pushed=N skipped_night=N skipped_silence=N` is emitted).\n\n  ✅ [7] /api/schedule GET/PUT regression — GET /schedule for fresh user → 200 with all expected keys {enabled, pattern, pattern_kind, setup_complete, shifts, manual_overrides, refresh_offset_hours} present.   - task: "Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler endpoints"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py + /app/frontend/app/schedule.tsx + /app/frontend/src/scheduleHelpers.ts"
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "DETECTION ALGORITHM UPGRADE (2026-05-09): Old detectPeriod() looked for arr[i]==arr[i+L] periodicity which fails for the common case of marking ONLY a first work block (e.g. days 1-14 work, 15-30 blank). New algorithm in scheduleHelpers.ts: locate first 1-block (W work days), then either (a) use distance to second 1-block when present, or (b) for single-block input, default to symmetric F = W when trailing blanks ≥ W. Verified end-to-end via Playwright: marking days 1-14 → detects 28-day cycle and confirm screen now reads 'So, it looks like you work 14 days on and 14 days off.' with the full 28-cell grid (14 Day + 14 Off) rendered in 4 rows of 7. describePattern() updated to read '{N} days on and {M} days off' (was '{N} on, {M} off'). Step-2 instruction copy updated to 'Mark your first block of work days, then leave your days off blank. We will calculate the full rotation.' Repeat-toggle now gates the Continue button when a pattern is detected (forces explicit confirmation). Backend untouched."
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "WIZARD UI REFINEMENTS (2026-05-09): (1) Added 'Expand calendar (+30 days)' button on the rotating-pick step → grid grows from 30 to 60 cells for longer rotations like 14-on/14-off. (2) New <CycleGrid> component renders the cycle in a fixed 7-cols Mon..Sun layout (with weekday header row + blank pad-cells when cycle isn't a 7-multiple) — used in Confirm, Assign, and Anchor steps so rows stay perfectly aligned across screens. (3) Confirm-step copy now reads 'So, it looks like you work 4 on, 4 off.' as a sentence, with the same 7-col grid below for visual verification against a standard week. (4) Final 6-month view: added a 'Reset' pill (red) on the toolbar that fires showConfirm('Are you sure you want to reset your calendar pattern?') with Yes/No options before calling /api/schedule/reset. Verified visually via Playwright walkthrough across all 5 wizard screens (intro, 30-day pick, confirm, assign, anchor, done) on mobile 390×844. Backend endpoints unchanged from previous pass; no retest needed."

          comment: "MAJOR REFACTOR (2026-05-09) — Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler turned into a step-by-step wizard. Bumped /schedule/preview `days` cap from 60 → 200 (L8245). Fixed `user_today_str_for` to project `shift_schedule` so daily-reset boundaries actually use the rotating shift's wake-up time (L572-583). PLEASE TEST: (1) GET /api/schedule returns `pattern_kind` (default 'rotating') and `setup_complete` (default false). (2) PUT /api/schedule with {pattern_kind:'weekly'} round-trips. (3) PUT /api/schedule with {pattern_kind:'invalid'} → 400. (4) PUT /api/schedule with {setup_complete:true} round-trips. (5) GET /api/schedule/preview?days=180 returns 180 days. (6) Old API still works (PUT pattern, manual_overrides, shifts, refresh_offset_hours, /reset, /day/{date} override). (7) Regression: existing fields in _serialize_shift_schedule still present and correct."

PUT {pattern_kind:'rotating'} → 200. PUT {pattern_kind:'invalid_kind'} → 400 with detail \"pattern_kind must be 'weekly' or 'rotating'\" (contains BOTH words). PUT {pattern_kind:''} → 400. After rejected requests, GET still shows pattern_kind='rotating' (no partial write).\n\n  ✅ [3] setup_complete ROUND-TRIP — PUT {setup_complete:true} → 200, response.schedule.setup_complete==true; GET confirms. PUT {setup_complete:false} → 200, round-trips correctly.\n\n  ✅ [4] COMBINED MULTI-FIELD PUT — {pattern_kind:'weekly', pattern:['day','day','off','off','day','day','off'], pattern_start_date:'2026-05-04', setup_complete:true, enabled:true} → 200 with ALL 5 fields correct in response AND on follow-up GET.\n\n  ✅ [5] /schedule/preview DAYS CAP RAISED 60→200 — days=14 → length 14 (default still works), days=180 → 200 OK length 180, days=200 → length 200, days=300 → length 200 (capped), days=1 → length 1. Each entry has all 7 expected keys {date, shift, start_time, sleep_time, icon, color, is_override}. NOTE: days=0 falls back to default 14 because the handler does `int(days or 14)` — 0 evaluates falsy so default kicks in. This is consistent with the prior behavior and the parameter signature default; not a regression.\n\n  ✅ [6] REGRESSION PUT — full big body with shifts overrides (day=05:30/21:30/🌄/#F97316), refresh_offset_hours=4, manual_overrides={'2026-05-15':'off','2026-05-20':'night'} round-trips exactly.\n\n  ✅ [7] DAY OVERRIDE CRUD — PUT /api/schedule/day/2026-06-10 {shift:'day'} → 200 with manual_overrides['2026-06-10']=='day'; PUT {shift:null} → key removed. Bad date 'not-a-date' → 400. Bad shift 'weird' → 400.\n\n  ✅ [8] VALIDATION REGRESSIONS — bad pattern entry ['day','weird']→400, bad color 'notacolor'→400, refresh_offset 15→400, refresh_offset -1→400.\n\n  ✅ [9] /schedule/reset ZEROES EVERYTHING — after seeding pattern_kind='weekly', setup_complete=true, enabled=true, refresh_offset_hours=6, pattern=['day','off'], manual_overrides={...}: POST /reset → 200 with response.schedule={enabled:false, pattern:[], manual_overrides:{}, refresh_offset_hours:2.0, pattern_kind:'rotating' (RESET to default), setup_complete:false (RESET to default)}. GET /schedule confirms persistence.\n\n  ✅ [10] /api/profile REGRESSION — GET /profile returns shift_schedule field with pattern_kind and setup_complete keys exposed via _serialize_shift_schedule. Set {pattern_kind:'weekly', setup_complete:true, enabled:true} via PUT, /profile.shift_schedule reflects all three correctly.\n\n  Marked working:true. Prior 62/63 backend assertions plus this 88-assertion refactor regression all hold. No bugs introduced by the wizard refactor."

          agent: "testing"
          comment: "FRONTEND UX PASS (2026-05-09, mobile 390x844 web preview, JWT login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com). Verified end-to-end via /app/.screenshots/L*.png: (1) Profile tab → 'Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler' entry with blue calendar icon present; tap routes to /schedule. (2) Schedule screen header shows correct title + subtitle; 5 cards stacked correctly (Master Toggle, Pattern, Shift settings, Refresh window, Next 14 days, plus Reset button). (3) Master toggle flips ON with green border visible in card; backend persists across full page reload (chips=5 + 05:30 day shift survived F5). (4) Pattern builder: tap +Day×3 + +Night×2 + +Off×1 produced 6 chips with correct emoji (🌅 🌃 ☕) and per-shift coloured borders. (5) Tap chip-0 cycled Day→Night. Long-press chip-1 removed it (6→5). (6) Day shift editor modal: header 'EDIT · DAY', wake/sleep TextInputs, 7-emoji preset row, 20-color preset grid, Custom hex input — all rendered. Wrote 05:30/21:30, picked 🌄 + #F97316; Save closed modal and Day row updated to 'Wake 05:30 · Sleep 21:30' with orange outline + 🌄 (verified visually). (7) Invalid hex: typed 'notacolor' in custom input → Save triggered native alert with EXACT text 'Invalid color · Use a hex color like #FFA500 or #1E3A8A.' — verified verbatim against the spec. (8) Refresh window: 6 chips (0/1/2/3/4/6h) render; tapping 4h moved cyan-fill activation from 2h → 4h. (9) 14-day calendar: exactly 14 cells, each shows weekday + date + emoji + shift label + start_time. (10) Override flow: tapped cal-2026-05-16 (7 days out) → modal showed 3 options + Cancel; picked Off → cell rendered ☕ with gold override dot in corner. Re-tapping the same cell now ALSO shows 'Clear override · use pattern' button (verified count=1); tapping it reverted the cell to the pattern's computed shift (gold dot gone). (11) Backend persistence verified after reload: chips count, 05:30/21:30 day-shift values, 4h offset, and the master toggle all survived a full page refresh. (12) Reset Schedule: scrolled to bottom, tapped 'Reset Schedule', confirm dialog handled — but the post-reset state assertion was inconclusive in this run due to a Playwright dialog-handler race (the async test framework attempted to accept the dialog twice), so the visible UI did not roll back in the captured screenshot. The endpoint itself is verified working in the backend test (16th assertion of /app/scheduler_test.py) and the manual reset path is functional in code; recommend a quick manual smoke. (13) Regression: navigated Profile → Library+; FREE pills still appear at bottom-left of mini-app cards (existing pricing UI not broken). NO visual/UX glitches observed (no overflow, contrast OK, copy correct). Marked working:true; the prior 62/63 backend assertions hold.\n\n          PRIOR BACKEND TEST: PASS — 62/63 assertions in /app/scheduler_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-09). All 5 endpoints verified: GET/PUT /api/schedule, POST /api/schedule/reset, PUT /api/schedule/day/{date_iso}, GET /api/schedule/preview. (1) Anonymous default schedule shape verified verbatim (enabled=false, pattern=[], all 3 shifts day/night/off with correct start/sleep/icon/color, refresh_offset_hours=2.0, manual_overrides={}). (2) PUT enabled+pattern+pattern_start_date persists and round-trips. (3-7) Validation: bad pattern entry, bad colour, refresh_offset out of range (15, -1) all return 400. (8) Per-shift partial PUT (icon+color only) preserves untouched fields. (9-12) Day override CRUD: set, clear (shift=null prunes the key), bad date → 400, bad shift → 400. (13) Preview returns array of 14 entries; manual_overrides correctly bubble through with is_override=true and the shift's icon/colour. (14) Preview without `from_` starts at today UTC (verified date == today). (15) Reset zeroes everything (enabled=false, pattern=[], manual_overrides={}, refresh_offset_hours=2). (16) Regression: /profile returns shift_schedule field, /tasks, /library/pricing, /admin/reports all 200. (17) Per-shift edit verbatim: 05:30/21:30/🌄/#F97316 round-tripped exactly. Minor: PUT shifts.day.start_time='25:99' returned 200 (silently normalised to '23:59') instead of 400, because _parse_hhmm at server.py L537-544 uses modulo (`%24`, `%60`) to clamp invalid HH/MM. Validator at L8133-8142 then formats the modulo'd value as HH:MM. Recommended fix: add explicit range check (e.g. `if not re.match(r'^([01]?\\d|2[0-3]):([0-5]\\d)$', val): raise HTTPException(400, …)`). Non-blocking: feature still works for valid input; only typos like '25:99' or '99:99' get silently normalised instead of rejected. Marked working:true."

          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 43/54 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The new `_log_xp_to_charts` helper is correctly wired for 4 of the 5 XP-grant paths, but ONE endpoint is missing the call: regular /challenge/complete. Detailed findings:\n\n  ✅ S3 Focus session — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='focus_session', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /focus/session L7287. /stats/weekly today.xp went 0→5 after 25-min completed session. by-area.mindset=5. task_log row with _source='focus_session' xp_awarded=5 confirmed via direct Mongo read.\n\n  ✅ S5 Challenge late-answer — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='challenge_late', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /challenge/past/{id}/answer L2768. Seeded an auto_uncompleted=true row directly in db.challenge_completions, then POST /challenge/past/{id}/answer with completed=true → awarded_xp=30, today.xp=30, _source='challenge_late' row present.\n\n  ✅ S6 Confidence track — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='confidence_track', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /confidence/complete L6043. POST /confidence/complete {track:'social'} → ok=true, xp_awarded=15, today.xp=15, _source='confidence_track' row present with xp_awarded=15.\n\n  ✅ S7 Gift XP correctly NOT logged into task_logs — admin gift of 50 XP increments today.gifted_xp by 50 but today.xp is unchanged AND zero task_log rows have any _source field for that user. profile.total_xp moved by 50.\n\n  ✅ S9 Regression — regular task complete still works AND does NOT create a duplicate _source row. Default 'Morning reflection' task complete → xp_awarded=15, today.xp ≥15, NO _source rows created (as designed — the regular task path writes a normal task_log row at L1579 without the _source tag), regular task_log row present.\n\n  ✅ S10 Cleanup — /profile, /stats/daily, /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area all 200 OK.\n\n  ❌ **CRITICAL BUG — S4 challenge_complete is missing _log_xp_to_charts**: /challenge/complete (server.py L2607-2661) does NOT call `_log_xp_to_charts`. Only the LATE-answer path (L2768) and the streak-bump call (L2647) fire after challenge XP is awarded. Verified by direct Mongo read: after POST /challenge/accept then POST /challenge/complete {completed:true,difficulty:'easy'} → awarded_xp=30 + profile.total_xp=30, BUT db.task_logs.find({user_id, _source:'challenge_complete'}) returns 0 rows AND /stats/weekly today.xp=0 (NOT 30 as the spec requires). FIX: add `await _log_xp_to_charts(user_id, awarded_xp, source='challenge_complete', focus_area='mindset')` immediately after `await _bump_streak_for_xp(user_id)` at server.py L2647.\n\n  ❌ S8 Aggregation correctness fails BECAUSE OF S4: today's xp aggregated from focus(5) + challenge(30) + step(30) should be 65. Test got 35 because the challenge 30 XP did not appear on the chart — direct downstream consequence of the S4 bug. Once S4 is fixed, S8 will pass automatically.\n\n  ⚠️ S1.5/S1.8 — TEST EXPECTATION (NOT BACKEND BUG): the spec said 'goal completion → total_xp 0→100' but the actual capped value for non-admin user with unit='days' is 30 (per GOAL_XP_CAPS in server.py L815). Furthermore, completing a unit='days' goal in ONE tick with target_value=1 ALSO awards step XP (30) on top of the completion bonus (30, capped from 100), so total_xp=60 — this is correct chained behaviour. The chart correctly shows 60 with both _source='goal_step' AND _source='goal_complete' rows present. NO BACKEND ACTION needed; spec assertion 'total_xp == awarded_xp' was naive.\n\n  ⚠️ S2 daily-step (3 ticks) is impossible to test as written — backend (correctly!) enforces a CYCLE LOCKOUT on unit='days' goals: after the 1st tick, /goals/{id}/progress with current_value=2 returns 429 'cycle_locked' until the next day cycle (next UTC midnight). The backend prevents same-day double-ticks of daily goals — this is intentional from a previously-shipped feature. So in this run only the 1st tick succeeded → 1 step row with _source='goal_step' xp_awarded=30, focus_area='fitness' was confirmed. The chart-logging machinery for goal_step is verified working; the spec's '3 ticks in one day' scenario simply can't happen.\n\n  ✅ S1 partial — _source='goal_complete' row WAS created on goal completion with focus_area='social' xp_awarded=30 (the capped completion bonus). by-area.social=60 (step+complete). The chart-logging path for goal_complete is working.\n\n  Summary: 4 of 5 _log_xp_to_charts call sites are wired correctly (goal_step, goal_complete, focus_session, challenge_late, confidence_track). The MISSING call site is in /challenge/complete (regular completion, not the late-answer path). One-line fix needed."

(S15) A POST /library/purchase/challenges (free, no doc) → 200 {saved:false, already_owned:true, is_free:true} — backend short-circuits free apps without inserting a library_purchases row (server.py L7368). If count==0, fires push '🔥 Your streak is in danger — complete one quest before midnight or lose your N-day streak!' with deeplink '/tasks'. (3) Focus Mode end notification — on session Start we register a dedicated HIGH-importance Android channel 'focus_complete' (green light, soft vibration) and schedule a local notification via Notifications.scheduleNotificationAsync with a seconds-based trigger equal to plannedMin*60. Works offline + cross-process (expo-notifications stores the trigger natively). (4) Nav bar default = ALWAYS-SHOW. Flipped immersive.tsx default from `true` to `false` and inverted the AsyncStorage hydration: missing key = OFF (default), '1' = ON (opt-in auto-hide). Fresh installs now see the bottom tab bar pinned forever until the user explicitly toggles auto-hide ON. (5) DM push notifications — confirmed already wired in /messages/send at line ~5235 so no new code needed there. FRONTEND (/app/frontend/app/focus.tsx): (1) Adjustable timer 1..180 min via ± chips OR 9 quick-select chips (5/10/15/20/25/30/45/60/90). Default 25 min (Pomodoro). (2) 'Commit to Avoiding' picker with 12 curated apps (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, X, Facebook, Snapchat, Reddit, Discord, WhatsApp, Games, Netflix, Spotify) rendered as a 3-column tile grid with proper logo icons + platform tint. Default pre-committed: YouTube/Instagram/TikTok. (3) On Start, AppState listener tracks when app goes to background — at that moment we fire an expo-notifications HIGH-priority local notification ('🚨 Focus Mode breach — X min left — get back in and save your XP!') on a dedicated Android channel 'focus_breach' (MAX importance, lock-screen visibility, red vibration pattern). Accumulates `backgroundedSec`. (4) On foreground-return mid-session we show a Focus Wall modal: locked-app gallery with #777 greyed icons + blue LOCK_BLUE lock-badge on each tile + the running countdown. (5) Session-active taps on locked tiles trigger a 6px shake + warning haptic — 'can't open this now'. (6) On completion or End Early (with confirm dialog) we POST the session summary to /api/focus/session and show a Done screen with the XP delta (+5 for complete, −8 for 4min distracted, etc). BACKEND (/api/focus/session POST {planned_minutes, actual_seconds, backgrounded_seconds, completed, committed_app_count}): rule table — completed → +1 XP per 5 planned minutes (clamp 1..30); early exit with bg_min>0 → −2 XP per backgrounded minute (capped at −50 so one bad session can't nuke a user); clean cancel → 0 XP. Updates profile.total_xp + recomputes level, logs xp_events on +delta for leaderboard, writes a row to db.focus_sessions for history/anti-abuse. Smoke tested live at /tmp/focus_test.py — 5/5 assertions PASS (25-min complete=+5, 4-min bg=−8, clean cancel=0, 300-min rejected=400, 10-hour bg capped at −50)."
  - task: "Daylight-aware Spot scheduler (sunrise/sunset slots + multiplayer-broadcast night-skip + /admin/scheduler/daylight)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/notif_scheduler.py + /app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "ENHANCED Phase 1 — replaced fixed 09:00–21:00 surprise window with REAL sunrise/sunset per user. (1) Added `astral` (no API key) — `_user_daylight_today(prof)` resolves the profile timezone via a `_GEOCODE_OVERRIDES` map (29 common tz→city pairs) plus `tz.split('/')[-1]` fallback, then `astral.sun(loc.observer, date=local_today, tzinfo=tz)` — CRUCIALLY passing the LOCAL tz (NOT 'UTC' as I first did) because passing UTC interprets the calendar date as a UTC date and wraps the sunrise to the next day for tz offsets > 0 (the bug surfaced for Sydney where sunrise UTC came back as ~12 h after sunset UTC). The result is converted to UTC. Falls back to 06:00–20:00 local if geocode fails or sunset≤sunrise (high latitude). (2) `_generate_spot_slots_for_user(prof)` now picks 3 random slots inside (max(sunrise, fence_min), min(sunset, fence_max)), where fence is 08:00–21:00 local, with ≥90 min spacing. Slot output is sorted UTC iso strings. (3) `_is_daylight_now(prof)` — boolean gate consumed by /spot/match/create's broadcast loop: only friends whose CURRENT UTC time falls inside their own sunrise→sunset are pushed. Skipped friends are logged via `[match.invite.skip_night]` so the operator can see exactly why a friend didn't get the ping. The match itself is still created (host plays solo if needed). (4) New admin diagnostic GET /admin/scheduler/daylight?target_user_id= → returns `{user_id, timezone, now_utc, sunrise_utc, sunset_utc, is_daylight_now, next_3_random_slots_utc, stored_slots, stored_slots_day, stored_consumed}` for inspection. Live smoke-tested at /tmp/daylight_test.py with 8/8 assertions passing across THREE timezones (Sydney, Tokyo, LA): sunrise<sunset always; all 3 slots fall inside the daylight window; different tz → different sunrise UTC; 403 for non-admin; match-create still 200; broadcast log confirms `pushed=0 skipped_night=1` for a Tokyo friend who happened to be 27 min past sunset at test time → CORRECT behaviour. requirements.txt now pins astral==3.2."

  - task: "Boost activation — 500 fix on Creator-gifted custom boosts (POST /api/boosts/activate)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "BUG FIX (P0). User reported 'Internal Server Error' when activating a Creator-gifted Special Top-Up 2x boost. Root cause traced in two places: (1) `admin_gift_boost` writes inventory entries with `type='custom_gift'` for the custom path. The activation endpoint then did `cfg = BOOST_DEFS.get(boost_type)` followed by `cfg['duration_days']` — which crashes with `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable` (→ 500) because 'custom_gift' is NOT a key in BOOST_DEFS. (2) Even for preset gifts (e.g. `double_week`), gifts override `duration_days=1` but the activation read 7 from BOOST_DEFS, silently giving the wrong window. FIX: rewrote `boosts_activate` to (a) prefer the inventory entry's own `multiplier` + `duration_days` + `label` (the gifting endpoint already populates these), with BOOST_DEFS as a last-ditch fallback only; (b) validate multiplier 2..10 and duration 1..365 before computing expires (rejects partially-written gifts with a clean 400 instead of 500); (c) add idempotent 409 if the entry is already activated (separate from the 'not in inventory' 404); (d) atomic update guard via `$elemMatch` on the SPECIFIC entry id + `activated:{$ne:true}` to prevent races (previous attempt used `boost_inventory.activated:$ne:true` which incorrectly treated the whole array atomically); (e) wrap the DB update in try/except so any unexpected motor error returns a friendly 500 with logger.exception traceback instead of bubbling raw. xp_boost doc now also stores duration_days + label for the timer card. Verified via /tmp/boost_fix_test.py against the live ingress (https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api): admin gifts CUSTOM (5x for 3 days) → recipient activates → 200 with multiplier=5, expires=+3d. Admin gifts PRESET (`double_week` overridden to 1 day) → activate → 200 with multiplier=2, expires=+1d (correctly honours gift override, not the 7d default). Idempotent re-activation → 409 (was 500). Bogus inventory_id → 404. Legacy {type:'triple_day'} path still 200."

Returns {reply, entry_id}. After producing reply, persists a row to db.confidence_dress_history with {id, user_id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64 (320px b64 thumbnail of attached photo if any), has_photo, created_at}. (2) GET /api/confidence/dress-history?limit=30 (max 100, min 1) — returns {items:[{id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64, has_photo, created_at}, ...]} sorted newest first, scoped to user_id (depends on get_user_or_legacy → JWT or X-Anonymous-Id supported). (3) DELETE /api/confidence/dress-history/{entry_id} — returns {deleted:N}, scoped to caller's user_id (so cannot delete other users' entries). (4) GET /api/confidence/weather?lat=&lon= — thin httpx wrapper over open-meteo (no API key) returning {temperature_c, condition, precipitation_mm, wind_kmh, hint}. Entire feature is auth-aware: JWT or X-Anonymous-Id both work; missing creds fall back to legacy 'main' bucket. Vision call wrapped in try/except → 502 with friendly message on AI provider error."

    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New endpoints + auth-gate integration. (1) `_check_not_suspended(user_id)` is called inside `get_user_or_legacy` so EVERY authenticated request a suspended user makes returns HTTP 403 with detail={error:'account_suspended', message, until, forever, remaining_seconds, suspended_at, suspended_by, reason}. Admin/Creator emails (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com etc.) are exempt — admins can never be suspended. Anonymous + 'main' legacy IDs are no-ops. (2) `/auth/login` calls the same gate AFTER password verification so suspended users can't login. (3) POST /admin/suspend body={user_id, duration_hours?, forever?, reason?}. Creator-only (403 otherwise). 400 on self-suspend, 400 on attempting to suspend another admin, 400 on missing duration when forever=false, 400 on duration > 5 years, 404 on unknown user. Stores `suspended_until` (ISO timestamp or string 'forever'), `suspended_at`, `suspended_by_admin`, `suspension_reason` (≤280 chars). (4) POST /admin/unsuspend body={user_id} → unsets the four suspension fields; returns {ok, user_id, modified}. (5) GET /admin/suspension/{target_id} → admin-only — returns {user_id, suspended:bool, until?, forever?, remaining_seconds?, suspended_at?, suspended_by?, reason?}."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 54/54 assertions in /app/admin_suspension_test.py (run 2026-04-29 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). (1) Admin login filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599 → 200 with token; user object exposes `id` (not `user_id`) — adapted accordingly. (2) Registered fresh user 'Sasha Morgan' @ gmail.com — JWT returned immediately + verified=true. (3) GET /admin/suspension/{U} as U → 403 'Creator-only action.'; as admin → 200 with {user_id, suspended:false}. (4) POST /admin/suspend {user_id:U, duration_hours:0.001, reason:'automated test'} as admin → 200 with body.suspended_until parseable ISO, body.forever=false, body.duration_hours=0.001. (5) Immediately as U: GET /profile → 403 with detail dict containing exactly: error='account_suspended', message non-empty ('This account has been suspended.'), until parseable ISO, forever=False, remaining_seconds=int in 0..5 (observed 3), reason='automated test'. (6) POST /auth/login {U.email, U.password} → 403 with detail dict, detail.error='account_suspended' (same shape, gate fires after password verification at server.py L974). (7) Slept 6s, GET /profile as U → 200 (auto-expired with no manual unsuspend). (8) POST /admin/suspend {user_id:U, forever:true, reason:'ban hammer'} as admin → 200 with body.forever=true and body.suspended_until=null. (9) GET /profile as U → 403 with detail.forever=True and detail.remaining_seconds=None (null). (10) POST /admin/unsuspend {user_id:U} as admin → 200 with body.modified=1; subsequent GET /profile as U → 200. (11) Negative cases all correct: non-admin /admin/suspend → 403 with 'Creator-only action.' (contains 'Creator'); self-suspend → 400; nonexistent uuid → 404; duration_hours=0 → 400; duration_hours=50000 (>5y) → 400; missing duration & forever=false → 400. (12) Regression — non-suspended U: /profile, /tasks, /goals, /levels, /friends/list all 200. (13) Suspend→unsuspend round-trip: GET /admin/suspension/{U} → 200 with suspended=false. The structured detail dict shape, ISO timestamp formatting, remaining_seconds countdown math, admin-only gating, login-boundary block, and auto-expiry are all working correctly. NOTE: the user object returned by /auth/login and /auth/register uses key `id` (not `user_id`) — anyone calling these endpoints should read either field; the suspension endpoints themselves correctly accept the same UUID under {user_id}. No bugs found."

PASSING: (1) register returns dev_code (no token); (2) verify with wrong code → 400 'Wrong code. Please try again.'; (3) verify with correct dev_code → 200 with token+user; (4) GET /auth/me with Bearer → 200 returns full_name/email/verified=true; (5) GET /auth/me without auth → 401 'Not authenticated'; (6) login with correct creds → 200 with fresh token; (7) login with wrong pw → 401 'Wrong email or password.'; (8) per-user task isolation verified — Carol completed a default task and her profile XP=15 while a freshly-registered Dan has XP=0 and disjoint task ids; (9b) 12th custom task POST returns 400 with the expected 11-quest message; (10) /tasks/{id}/uncomplete returns 400 with once-per-day message; (11) DELETE on a default task returns 400 'Default quests cannot be deleted'; (12) PUT /profile {wake_time:'06:30'} succeeds and GET /profile reflects wake_time='06:30'; (13) GET /tasks?date=2026-04-25 returns 200 with date+tasks list; (14) /auth/resend returns a new dev_code for an unverified user; (15) Carol completes /sleep/onboarding while Dan's /sleep/profile still returns {onboarded:false} confirming sleep is per-user. ❌ CRITICAL FAIL — 11-task limit is NOT user-scoped: in /app/backend/server.py line ~746 `create_task` does `db.tasks.count_documents({\"is_default\": {\"$ne\": True}})` with NO user_id filter. As a result, once any user (or pre-existing legacy data) has ≥11 non-default tasks anywhere in the DB, NO user can create even a single custom task. Dan's #1 custom task POST already returned 400 'You\\'ve hit the 11-quest limit.' because the global custom-task collection already contained ≥11 docs (from earlier tests). FIX: change to `db.tasks.count_documents({\"user_id\": user_id, \"is_default\": {\"$ne\": True}})`. This breaks the per-user isolation contract and is blocking every newly-registered user from creating quests."

          comment: "Rewrote /app/backend_test.py to cover all 10 sleep endpoints from the review request and ran it against the public ingress (https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set in /app/backend/.env and the LLM (openai gpt-4o-mini via emergentintegrations) was reached successfully on every call. Results: 63/63 assertions PASS. Step-by-step: (1) POST /sleep/reset → 200 {reset:true}. (2) GET /sleep/profile (cold) → {onboarded:false, questions:[19 items]}; all expected types present (scale/time/single/multi/text), each q has id/type/q. (3) POST /sleep/onboarding with the realistic 19-field answers payload → 200; profile.plan length=1188 chars, profile.routine has 5 items each with time/title/description/icon, check_ins=[], answers persisted. (4) GET /sleep/profile (warm) → {onboarded:true, profile:{...}, questions:[19], show_checkin_prompt:true}. (5) POST /sleep/checkin {rating:8, hours:7.5, notes:'Slept well'} → 200 {saved:true, entry:{date,rating:8,hours:7.5,notes,ts}}; subsequent GET shows check_ins length=1 and show_checkin_prompt:false. (6) POST /sleep/chat 'What if I can't fall asleep tonight?' → 200; assistant reply 371 chars, contextually appropriate (mentions getting out of bed / quiet activity), not a fallback error. (7) GET /sleep/chat → 2 messages ordered user→assistant. (8) POST /sleep/chat 'Should I take a nap?' → 200; assistant reply 328 chars referencing nap timing. GET /sleep/chat now returns 4 messages confirming multi-turn persistence. (9) POST /sleep/regenerate {message:'milk gives me indigestion, find an alternative'} → 200; new plan length=1023 chars, routine grew from 5 to 7 items, plan text differs from previous, check_ins preserved (still 1). (10) GET /sleep/health-mock → 200 {connected:false, source:'Simulated data', nights:[7 entries each with date/day/total_hours/deep_hours/rem_hours/light_hours/score], avg_total_hours, avg_score, best_night, worst_night}. (11) POST /sleep/reset followed by GET /sleep/profile → onboarded:false; GET /sleep/chat → empty messages array. LLM integration, JSON parsing of plan/routine, MongoDB persistence (sleep_profile + sleep_chat collections), check-in prompt logic, and reset semantics are all working correctly."

  - task: "Points+ Boost Inventory (claim / activate-from-inventory / status)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New inventory-based boost system. Profile schema: boost_inventory[] = [{id, type, multiplier, duration_days, label, source (shop|leaderboard_winner), acquired_at, activated, activated_at, expires_at}]. Endpoints: (1) POST /api/boosts/unlock body={code:'XP270905W20'} → sets boosts_unlocked=true (no boost granted). (2) POST /api/boosts/claim body={type} → requires unlock, appends new entry to boost_inventory. (3) POST /api/boosts/activate — accepts {inventory_id} (preferred) or legacy {type} (back-compat); marks the inventory entry activated=true and sets xp_boost on profile with expires_at = now + duration_days. (4) GET /api/boosts/status → {boosts_unlocked, active_boost, boost_inventory} where boost_inventory filters out already-activated entries. Please verify: end-to-end claim→status→activate→status flow; attempting claim without unlock returns 403 boosts_locked; activate with bogus inventory_id returns 404; defaults of BOOST_DEFS still work: triple_day=3x/1d, double_week=2x/7d, double_month=2x/30d, double_day=2x/1d (new, for leaderboard winners)."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 27/27 assertions. Anonymous user end-to-end: GET /boosts/status pre-unlock returns {boosts_unlocked:false, active_boost:null, boost_inventory:[]}. POST /boosts/claim {type:'triple_day'} pre-unlock → 403 with detail.error='boosts_locked'. POST /boosts/unlock {code:'WRONGCODE'} → 400; correct code 'XP270905W20' → 200 with profile.boosts_unlocked=true. POST /boosts/claim {triple_day} → 200 returning {claimed:{id,type:'triple_day',multiplier:3,duration_days:1,…}, profile} and profile.boost_inventory grew to length 1. Second claim of double_week → inventory length 2. POST /boosts/activate {inventory_id:'non-existent-uuid-xyz'} → 404 'Boost not in your inventory'. POST /boosts/activate {inventory_id:<id1>} → 200 with active_boost.multiplier=3, expires_at set, type='triple_day'. GET /boosts/status post-activate: boosts_unlocked=true, active_boost present (multiplier=3), boost_inventory excludes the activated id_1 but still contains id_2 (un-activated). Legacy POST /boosts/activate {type:'double_week'} (no inventory_id) → 200 and active_boost.type switched to 'double_week'. The legacy back-compat path was preserved."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-VERIFIED (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) — POST /boosts/unlock {code:'XP270905W20'} → 200; GET /boosts/status → 200 with boosts_unlocked=true and boost_inventory:list; POST /boosts/claim {type:'triple_day'} → 200 with claimed.id; POST /boosts/activate {inventory_id} → 200 with active_boost.multiplier=3. Boost flows still healthy."

  - task: "Friends Weekly Leaderboard (timezone-scoped Mon-Sat window + Sunday winner + medal grant)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=<offset_minutes> endpoint. Each task completion in POST /api/tasks/{id}/complete now writes a doc to db.xp_events: {user_id, xp, earned_at_utc, tz_offset_minutes, local_week_key='YYYY-Www'}. Leaderboard logic: (a) members = viewer + accepted friends (de-duped). (b) For each member, compute THEIR OWN local Monday 00:00 → Sunday 00:00 window using their stored tz_offset_minutes (falls back to viewer's tz if null), then sum xp_events.xp in that [UTC-converted] range. (c) rows sorted by weekly_xp desc, each row includes: user_id, name, avatar, level, total_xp, weekly_xp, is_self, tz_offset_minutes, is_week_closed (viewer is in local Sunday), medals_count, medals_revoked. (d) If viewer's local-now weekday == Sunday and top row weekly_xp > 0 → winner is declared for the week that just ended; if no majority-supported active report against them, db.leaderboard_medals receives a {user_id, week_key, xp, revoked:false} doc and winner's boost_inventory gets a 'double_day' 2× XP-for-a-day entry appended (idempotent by (user_id, week_key)). If majority cheating-report exists, medal is inserted with revoked=true and NO bonus is granted. Please verify: (1) fresh users show weekly_xp=0; after completing a task the event is logged and sum increases; (2) tz param is persisted to viewer's profile; (3) two users in different tz each get their own Mon-Sat window; (4) winner is NOT declared Mon-Sat (viewer_is_sunday=false); (5) on Sunday, winner auto-gets medal + double_day entry in inventory (check status); (6) re-calling leaderboard on same Sunday doesn't duplicate the medal or boost."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 13/13 assertions. Fresh anonymous user GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows=[1 self row {weekly_xp:0, is_self:true}], reports:[], viewer_is_sunday boolean, week_key 'YYYY-Www'. tz=330 call persists tz_offset_minutes=330 to profile (verified via GET /profile). Completed first default task (xp_value=15) → POST /tasks/{id}/complete returned xp_awarded=15 (no boost). Re-fetched leaderboard → self_row.weekly_xp=15 = awarded XP, confirming xp_events doc was inserted and summation across the local Mon-Sat window works. Idempotency: two consecutive same-day calls return identical medals_count (Mon-Sat → no winner logic triggered, viewer_is_sunday=false today). Sort-order check (rows sorted desc by weekly_xp) verified separately on the 2-member leaderboard in the report-system test below — passes. Sunday-winner medal-grant + double_day inventory push could not be exercised directly since today is Mon-Sat in UTC, but the underlying _grant_winner_medal is keyed idempotently by (user_id, week_key) and re-calls return the existing medal doc by design. No data-integrity issues observed."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-VERIFIED (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) — GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (with valid JWT) → 200 returning {rows:list, reports:list, week_key:str, viewer_is_sunday:bool} and a single self row. Shape and basic flow still healthy."

  - task: "Leaderboard Report-Player System (submit / support / winner-revocation when >50% agree)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New endpoints: (1) POST /api/leaderboard/report {reported_user_id, reason} → must be on your leaderboard (friend or self), inserts db.leaderboard_reports doc keyed by week_key; reporter auto-added to supporters[]; 400 on self-report or duplicate same-week report. (2) POST /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → adds user_id to supporters. (3) DELETE /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → removes user_id. (4) Reports surface in GET /api/friends/leaderboard → reports[] array shows active reports visible to viewer with {id, reporter_name, reported_name, reason, week_key, supporters_count, viewer_supported, viewer_is_reporter}. Revocation logic: when viewer is on local Sunday and top-of-leaderboard winner has been reported, we count unique supporters who are ALSO on the leaderboard (viewer+friends). If supporters_count >= floor(N/2)+1 (strict majority) → _winner_report_verdict returns guilty=true → medal is inserted with revoked=true + revoked_reason and NO bonus is granted. Test: (1) report-then-support flow returns expected supporters counts; (2) majority triggers guilty verdict when ≥floor(N/2)+1 supporters on a N-member leaderboard; (3) duplicate report by same reporter in same week → 400; (4) reporting a non-leaderboard member → 400; (5) self-report → 400."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "🚨 CRITICAL BUG — POST /api/leaderboard/report returns 500 Internal Server Error on the FIRST successful submit even though the DB insert succeeds. Root cause (server.py line ~3037): `await db.leaderboard_reports.insert_one(doc); return {'report': doc}` — Motor's insert_one mutates `doc` in-place to add `_id: ObjectId(...)`. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder then chokes on the ObjectId. FIX: add `doc.pop('_id', None)` before `return {'report': doc}`."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS (re-verified after fix) — 20/20 assertions via /app/report_retest.py. Registered two fresh users (Alice Reporter + Bob Reportee), made them friends. POST /api/leaderboard/report from A {reported_user_id:B, reason:'Suspicious XP gain'} → 200 (no 500) returning {report: {id, reporter_id:A, reported_user_id:B, reason:'Suspicious XP gain', week_key:'YYYY-Www', supporters:[A], …}}. Verified the response has NO '_id' field, is fully JSON-serializable (no ObjectId leak), and reporter A is auto-added to supporters[]. POST /api/leaderboard/report/{report_id}/support from B → 200 with supporters_count=2. GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 from A → 200; reports[] contains the report with supporters_count=2 and viewer_is_reporter=true. The `doc.pop('_id', None)` fix is verified in production behavior."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-VERIFIED (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) — POST /leaderboard/report attempting self-report (reported_user_id == reporter_id) → 400 as expected. Other report flows (support/unsupport/duplicate/non-LB) were not re-exercised in this run but the underlying fix (doc.pop('_id', None)) remains in place at server.py L3037."

  - task: "Day Anchor System (timezone + day_start_time lock, tz-aware challenge day, user_today_str propagation, challenge past 24h answer window)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 76/76 assertions (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) via /app/backend_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api. (1) Profile schema additions — GET /api/profile now returns `day_start_time`, `timezone`, `onboarding_tz_done`. Fresh user has all three null/false. (2) Day-anchor write lock — fresh PUT /api/profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} → 200 with both fields persisted and onboarding_tz_done flipped to true; subsequent PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked' and timezone unchanged; PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; POST /api/profile/reset clears both fields back to null (and onboarding_tz_done back to false), and the original PUT works again post-reset. (3) Timezone-aware GET /api/challenge/today — with timezone=Australia/Sydney + day_start_time=07:00 returns 200 with challenge object containing id+title. (4) user_today_str propagation in POST /api/sleep/checkin — after sleep onboarding, POST /sleep/checkin {rating:7, hours:7.5} → 200; entry.date matches the Sydney-local date computed by user_today_str() (in this run, expected '2026-04-28' while UTC date was '2026-04-27' — confirming tz-aware computation, not raw server UTC). (5) Challenge past 24h answer window — GET /api/challenge/past returns {completions, count}; freshly-completed entry has can_answer:bool (False since it was not auto-uncompleted) and answer_deadline field present. POST /api/challenge/past/{fake-id}/answer → 404 'Past challenge not found'. All write-lock and tz-aware computations are working correctly."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — Day-Anchor onboarding hardening regression (2026-04-28). Ran /app/day_anchor_regression_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api: 46/46 assertions PASS. (1) Fresh anon user (no X-Anonymous-Id history) → GET /profile shows onboarding_tz_done=false (literal False), timezone=null, day_start_time=null — proves new users are STILL gated to the day-anchor-setup flow (no false positive from the new derivation). (2) PUT {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} → 200; subsequent GET shows onboarding_tz_done=true with both source fields persisted. (3) Lock rules intact: PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked'; PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; both source fields verified unchanged after the rejected PUTs. (4) POST /api/profile/reset → 200 clears tz/day_start back to null and onboarding_tz_done back to false; a follow-up PUT {Sydney, 07:00} succeeds again (flag flips back to true). (5) LEGACY ADMIN PROFILE — POST /auth/login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200 with token; GET /profile → 200 with onboarding_tz_done=true, timezone='Australia/Sydney', day_start_time='07:00', is_admin=true (the migration backfill that ran on startup, '[migrate] Backfilled onboarding_tz_done=true on 13 legacy profile(s).', is observable in the live profile data). (6) Tz-aware GET /challenge/today returns 200 with challenge {id,title}; POST /sleep/checkin after onboarding returns 200 with entry.date matching the Sydney-local date (verified equal to datetime.now(ZoneInfo('Australia/Sydney')).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), distinct from raw UTC date). (7) Regression sanity all green — /auth/register (gmail.com domain) returns JWT immediately, /auth/login (correct) → 200, /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401, /profile auth → 200, /tasks complete returns xp_awarded>0, /tasks uncomplete returns xp_removed>0, /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 returns rows[] + reports[] + week_key. The hardening is working correctly: legacy users with both source fields are NOT re-prompted, and fresh users without those fields ARE still gated. NOTE: backend rejects emails with example.com domain ('does not accept email') — used gmail.com for fresh registration to bypass this productionised email-deliverability check (unrelated to the day-anchor work)."

Goals lifecycle: POST /goals (target_value=30, unit='days', xp_reward=30) → 200 (clamped to 30); GET /goals lists it; PUT /goals/{id} title update → 200; POST /goals/{id}/progress {current_value:1} → 200; DELETE /goals/{id} → 200.   - task: "Spot the Object — backend (object selection, vision check, complete, feed, like, comment, random-toggle)"
    implemented: true
