    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 104/105 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (the 1 minor non-spot self-assertion is unrelated). Tested end-to-end against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api with anonymous mode (X-Anonymous-Id 36-char ids). Real photos pulled from loremflickr.com (keyword-relevant Flickr JPEGs, ~20KB each, satisfying /app/image_testing.md no-blank-images rule).\n\n  ✅ Profile additions: GET /api/profile fresh user → spot_points=0 (int), spot_random_enabled=False (bool). After /spot/complete success=true, spot_points=1.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/object: returns {object, challenge_id} with object always in the curated SPOT_OBJECTS list. 8 calls produced 8 distinct objects ('anything green', 'cat', 'chair', 'coin', 'flower', 'mug', 'pair of glasses', 'window') — randomization works.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/check: empty photo→400, >8MB b64→400. Real-photo positive case (leaf photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=True, confidence=1.0, can_capture=True. Real-photo negative case (chair photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=False, confidence=0.0, can_capture=False. can_capture invariant `detected AND confidence>=0.55` holds. GPT-4o-mini Vision integration via emergentintegrations is working — the LLM returned correct strict JSON for both polarity tests.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/complete: success=true → {entry, points_delta:1, spot_points:1, profile} with profile.spot_points incremented. success=false → points_delta=0, spot_points unchanged. Entry has id (uuid), success, target_object, mode preserved.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/feed?limit=50: fresh user → entries:[]. After 2 completions → 2 entries each with player_name, player_avatar_base64, player_spot_points, liked_by_you, like_count, comment_count, is_self all populated; is_self=True for self.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/like: toggle 0→1→0 with liked_by_you flipping accordingly.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/comment: whitespace-only text→400 'Comment can\\'t be empty'. Normal comment 'Beautiful find! 🌿' → 200 with comment doc {id, user_id, user_name, user_avatar_base64, text, created_at}. Long text (350 chars) → 200 with text truncated to exactly 280 chars (no error).\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/{id}: returns full detail including comments[] (length=2 after 2 inserts) plus player_* and like_* fields. Bogus id → 404 'Photo not found'.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/random-toggle: {enabled:true} → response shows spot_random_enabled=True; subsequent GET /api/profile reflects True. {enabled:false} → reflected back to False on both response and GET /api/profile.\n\n  ✅ Regression: /api/profile, /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (rows/reports/week_key/viewer_is_sunday + self row), /api/tasks complete+uncomplete cycle (xp_awarded=15 then xp_removed=15) — all healthy. Boost system, auth, sleep, day-anchor not re-run as instructed.\n\n  No bugs found. Vision API was fully reachable; both positive and negative cases produced correct LLM output."

        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 8/8 assertions. From registered user A's JWT, GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{B_id}?tz=0 → 200 returning the standard _serialize_player payload (user_id matches B, name='Bob Reportee', level int, total_xp int, friend_status='friends') PLUS the leaderboard-specific extensions: weekly_xp:int, medals:[] (empty for fresh user), is_flagged_cheater:false. GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{random-uuid}?tz=0 → 404 'Player not found'. The medal-attached / revoked path could not be triggered directly (today is Mon-Sat UTC), but the implementation reads from db.leaderboard_medals correctly via _compute_medals() and is_flagged_cheater = any(m.revoked for m in medals) is straightforward and correct by inspection."

  - task: "last_seen_at + UX-fix regression (friends list, players search, player detail, throttle, no-auth fallback, full critical-path)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS with one MINOR gap — 65/68 assertions in /app/last_seen_regression_test.py (run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The 3 failures are all the same single gap below.\n\n  ✅ [last_seen on friends list — PRIMARY UX SURFACE] Registered Nina Chen + Oliver Park, A→B friend request via POST /friends/request {user_id:B}, B accepts via POST /friends/accept {user_id:A}. GET /friends/list as A → 200 with friends[] containing B; B's entry has last_seen_at present and an ISO-8601 string (parses cleanly with datetime.fromisoformat). Mirror direction also works: GET /friends/list as B has A's entry with last_seen_at populated. ✅\n\n  ✅ [last_seen on player search + detail] GET /friends/players?q=Oliver → 200 with B in results; result has last_seen_at key. GET /friends/profile/{B_id} → 200 returning standard _serialize_player payload including last_seen_at. ✅\n\n  ✅ [Throttle correctness — performance] Rapid-fire GET /api/profile 10x within ~1s: all 200, zero errors, last_seen_at value remained STABLE across all 10 calls (1 distinct value), proving the in-process _LAST_SEEN_THROTTLE (60s window) is correctly suppressing redundant DB writes. ✅\n\n  ✅ [No-auth fallback] GET /api/profile with neither Bearer nor X-Anonymous-Id → 200 (no crash). _touch_last_seen('main') executes silently inside get_user_or_legacy → does not throw. Legacy 'main' profile is returned. ✅\n\n  ✅ [Critical-path regression — entire flow green]: POST /auth/register (gmail.com) returns JWT immediately + verified=true; POST /auth/login (correct creds) → 200; POST /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401. PUT /profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} on fresh profile → 200 with onboarding_tz_done=true; PUT timezone again → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked'; PUT day_start_time again → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; POST /profile/reset → 200 clears both; subsequent PUT works again. POST /tasks/{id}/complete with body {date:today} → 200 returning xp_awarded=15; profile.total_xp grew 0→15; POST /tasks/{id}/uncomplete → 200 returning xp_removed=15; profile.total_xp rolled back to 0. POST /goals (focus_area=fitness, target_value=30, unit='days', xp_reward=30) → 200; PUT /goals/{id} → 200; DELETE /goals/{id} → 200. GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows[]+reports[]+week_key+viewer_is_sunday. GET /spot/object → 200 with {object,challenge_id}; POST /spot/check (empty photo) → 400. Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200, /profile shows is_admin=true. ✅\n\n  ⚠️ MINOR — /api/profile (the user's OWN profile) does NOT expose `last_seen_at` in its response. Root cause: server.py L477 `serialize_profile()` was NOT updated when `_serialize_player()` (L2630) gained the field. _touch_last_seen DOES correctly write profile.last_seen_at to MongoDB on every request (verified — friends/list and player detail surface a fresh ISO timestamp), but the user's self-profile dict simply omits the field. This does NOT block the actual UX feature (the 'Last seen X hrs ago' label in Friends/index PlayerCard reads from /friends/list, which DOES expose last_seen_at correctly). Optional 1-line fix in serialize_profile: `\"last_seen_at\": prof.get(\"last_seen_at\")`. Marking working:true because (a) the primary UX surface (friends list + player profiles) works end-to-end, (b) no 500 on /profile, (c) no data corruption, (d) throttle works correctly, (e) no-auth fallback works. The /api/profile gap is a cosmetic omission, not a functional break."

Registered 3 fresh users (Olivia Carter / Marcus Bell / Sophia Reyes via gmail.com), made Host↔Friend1 friends via POST /friends/request {user_id:F1} → POST /friends/accept {user_id:Host}, left Host↔Friend2 strangers.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/create] (a) friend_ids=[] → 400 detail='Pick at least one friend to invite.' (b) friend_ids=[stranger only] → 400 detail='No confirmed friends in the invite list.' (c) friend_ids=[Friend1, Friend2_stranger] → 200; match.players contains Host+Friend1 only (Friend2 filtered out by friend_requests status=accepted check at server.py L3814-3822); status='waiting'; target_object=null.\n\n  ✅ [SHAPE check — every key the frontend types expect] match dict has id, host_id, status, target_object, started_at, ends_at, finished_at, seconds_left, winner_id, players[], viewer_role, viewer_captures, created_at. Each player dict has user_id, name, avatar_base64, is_host, joined, declined, captures. (Note: the review request mentions viewer_reward — server.py L3723 ONLY sets viewer_reward when status=='finished', otherwise it is omitted entirely from the dict. This is by design since viewer_reward is irrelevant pre-finalize. If the frontend expects it always-present, consider returning 0 instead of omitting — but it does not affect functionality.)\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/list] Host sees the new waiting match. Friend1 sees it (as invitee). Friend2 does NOT see it (was correctly filtered at create).\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/join] Friend2 (uninvited) → 403. Friend1 → 200 with match.players[Friend1].joined=true. Friend1 again → idempotent 200 (no error, $addToSet keeps it idempotent).\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/start] Friend1 → 403 detail='Only the host can start the match.' Host → 200 with status='active', target_object='leaf' (from SPOT_OBJECTS), started_at + ends_at ISO strings populated, seconds_left=120 (in 110-120 range as required). Host start AGAIN → 400 detail='Match is not in a startable state.'\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/capture] Empty photo → 400. >8MB → 400. Friend2 (not in match) → 403. Host capture with REAL Flickr JPEG (loremflickr.com, ~20KB) → 200 with shape {detected:bool, confidence:float, can_capture:bool, captures:int, match:{...}}. can_capture invariant `detected AND confidence>=0.55` correctly enforced. In this run target='leaf' and the LLM happened to return detected=False/confidence=0.0 for that particular Flickr leaf image, so captures correctly stayed at 0 (confirms the conditional-increment branch — the endpoint shape and contract are verified).\n\n  ✅ [Auto-finalize / timer reality] Two GETs spaced 3.5 s apart on the same active match showed seconds_left counted down by ≥2 s (e.g. 119 → 116). Confirms ends_at is a real wall-clock deadline. Did not blocking-wait 2 minutes for the lazy finalize path; review request explicitly accepted the timer-count-down check as sufficient.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/cancel] Created 2nd waiting match. Friend1 → 403. Host → 200 {ok:true}. Host /start on the cancelled match → 400.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/decline] Created 3rd waiting match. Friend1 /decline → 200 {ok:true}. Friend1 /list no longer contains that match (decline removed Friend1 from invited+joined arrays).\n\n  ✅ [REGRESSION on Phase 1 /api/spot/*] /spot/object → 200 with {object, challenge_id}. /spot/check with real Flickr leaf JPG → 200 with detected/confidence/can_capture shape (vision returned detected=False on this run's particular image, but contract correct). /spot/complete success=true → 200 with points_delta=1; /profile.spot_points correctly incremented by 1. /spot/feed → 200 with entries[]. /spot/random-toggle enabled=true → spot_random_enabled=true; enabled=false → false.\n\n  No bugs found. All 7 new /api/spot/match/* endpoints + lazy auto-finalize behave exactly as specified. Phase 1 endpoints unaffected."

  - task: "Direct Messaging + AI safety guard + Admin reports (9 new endpoints in server.py L4055-4424)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 88/88 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-04-29 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered 3 fresh users (Alice Morgan, Bob Carter, Carol Diaz @ gmail.com). Friend pair Alice↔Bob established via POST /friends/request + POST /friends/accept (note: server exposes /friends/accept, not /friends/respond as stated in the review request — but functionally equivalent). Carol left as a stranger.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/refine] (a) 'hey hows it going lol' → 200 with refined non-empty, severity='none', flagged=false. (b) empty → 200 with refined='', severity='none'. (c) 'shit dude this sucks' → 200 with severity='mild' and refined string (LLM scrubbed profanity). (d) 'send me nudes right now baby' → 200 with severity='severe', refined='', flagged=true. GPT-4o-mini via emergentintegrations is reachable; the JSON schema is correctly parsed by _refine_message_text().\n\n  ✅ [/messages/send] (a) Alice→Bob 'Hey Bob! How's it going?' → 200 with message {id, from_user_id=Alice, to_user_id=Bob, text non-empty, severity='none', read_at=null}. (b) Alice→Carol stranger → 403 'You can only message friends.' (c) Alice→Alice self → 400 'Invalid recipient.' (d) Alice→Bob with empty refined_text → 400 'Message is empty.' (e) Alice→Bob with predatory text 'send me nude pics right now baby' → 400 with detail={'error': 'blocked', ...} AND a row appeared in db.admin_reports for Alice (kind='message_text', severity='severe', viewed_at=null, dismissed_at=null) — verified through the admin endpoint below.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/threads] After Alice's good send: Alice GET → threads:[{friend_id:Bob, friend_name='Bob Carter', last_message:{...}, unread_count:0}]. Bob GET → unread_count=1, last_message.text matches what Alice sent. Carol GET → threads:[].\n\n  ✅ [/messages/thread/{friend_id}] Bob GET /messages/thread/{Alice.user_id} → 200 with messages:[{id, text, …}] in chronological order (single msg this point). Bob GET /messages/thread/{Carol.user_id} → 403 'Not friends.'\n\n  ✅ [/messages/read] Bob POST /messages/read {friend_id:Alice} → 200 with updated=1. Bob's subsequent /messages/threads shows Alice's unread_count=0. Bob POST /messages/read {friend_id:Carol} → 403.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/unread-summary] Alice sends 2 more messages to Bob → Bob GET /messages/unread-summary → 200 with unread_by_friend={<Alice.id>:2}, total_unread=2.\n\n  ✅ [/push/register-token] Bob POST {token:'ExponentPushToken[fakebob]', platform:'android'} → 200 ok:true. Same token re-POST → 200 (upsert idempotent). Empty token → 400 'token required'. Anonymous POST (no auth, no anon-id) → 200 ok:true (binds to legacy 'main').\n\n  ✅ [/messages/check-image] Real loremflickr leaf JPEG (~8KB b64) → 200 with safe=True, severity='none', reason populated. image_base64='' → 400. >8MB string → 400.\n\n  ✅ [Admin endpoints] POST /auth/login {filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599} → 200 with admin token. Admin GET /admin/reports → 200 with {reports:list, new_count:int}. The severe message_text report on Alice from step 2e is present (severity='severe', kind='message_text', reported_user_id=Alice, viewed_at=null, dismissed_at=null). Bob (non-admin) GET /admin/reports → 403 'Admin only.' Admin POST /admin/reports/{id}/view → 200; subsequent GET shows viewed_at=ISO timestamp populated AND new_count decremented (1→0). Admin POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss → 200; subsequent GET no longer includes the dismissed report (filter dismissed_at:null is enforced).\n\n  ✅ [Regression] (a) /auth/login (correct creds) → 200; wrong pw → 401. (b) /api/profile includes both last_seen_at AND onboarding_tz_done keys. (c) /api/spot/match/* smoke run all 200 (create with 1 friend, list, get, cancel). (d) /api/friends/list entry has last_seen_at field populated. (e) /challenge/today: today's deterministic per-user quote was 'He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.' (Nietzsche), so 'is NOW.' did not surface in this run, but verified the source string 'is NOW.' IS present at challenges_data.py:513 — the quote rotation is working.\n\n  No bugs found. All 9 new DM/admin endpoints work as specified, AI safety guard correctly identifies severity='severe' content and files admin reports, defence-in-depth re-pass on /send blocks predatory content even if a malicious client sends a non-refined string, and friends-only gate is enforced on every messaging route. NOTE: review request mentioned /friends/respond — actual route is /friends/accept (existing endpoint), tested with that. NOTE: review mentioned 'NOW' uppercase quote — confirmed present in challenges_data.py:513; whether it surfaces on a given /challenge/today call depends on per-user-per-day deterministic rotation."

metadata:
  created_by: "testing_agent"
  version: "1.6"
  test_sequence: 7
  run_ui: false

test_plan:
  current_focus:
    - "Goal time-lock (first tick) + Goal XP add/refund on tick/untick + goal_xp chart segment"
  stuck_tasks:
    - "Goal time-lock (first tick) + Goal XP add/refund on tick/untick + goal_xp chart segment"
  test_all: false
  test_priority: "high_first"

agent_communication:
    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler. profile.shift_schedule overrides the static day_start_time when enabled. user_today_str() now uses the per-day shift's wake-up time so daily resets follow the user's pattern. Endpoints (server.py): GET /api/schedule, PUT /api/schedule (validates HH:MM, hex #RRGGBB, percent ranges, ISO dates), POST /api/schedule/reset, PUT /api/schedule/day/{date_iso} {shift:'day'|'night'|'off'|null}, GET /api/schedule/preview?days=N. TEST PLAN: (1) anon GET /api/schedule → 200 default. (2) PUT pattern=['day','day','night','night','off','off','off','off'] enabled=true → 200. (3) invalid pattern entry → 400. (4) bad color #ZZZZZZ → 400. (5) refresh_offset_hours=15 → 400. (6) shift start_time='25:99' → 400. (7) PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-09 {shift:'off'} → 200, manual_overrides has entry. (8) PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-09 {shift:null} → 200, cleared. (9) bad date 'foo' → 400. (10) bad shift 'lunch' → 400. (11) GET /schedule/preview?days=14 → 200 array len 14. (12) After step 7, preview shows date='2026-05-09' is_override=true shift='off'. (13) POST /schedule/reset → 200; subsequent GET enabled=false pattern=[] manual_overrides={} refresh_offset_hours=2. (14) Regression: existing /profile, /tasks, /goals, /library/pricing, /admin/reports still work. Mark working:true if all pass."

  - task: "Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler endpoints"
    implemented: true
    working: "NA"
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Implemented in server.py: DEFAULT_SHIFTS, SHIFT_TYPES, helpers (_shift_for_date, _effective_day_start_for, _is_in_silence_window, _serialize_shift_schedule, _validate_shift_schedule, _default_shift_schedule, _parse_hhmm) + 5 endpoints. user_today_str now delegates to _effective_day_start_for so the daily-reset boundary follows the per-day shift wake-up time when enabled. profile serializer exposes shift_schedule. Frontend: app/schedule.tsx (Master Toggle, pattern builder, per-shift editor with emoji/color picker + custom hex, refresh offset chips, 14-day calendar preview with override modal, Reset Schedule button). Profile screen has new 'Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler' entry routing to /schedule."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Boost pricing endpoints + payment-intent kind='boost' — 63/63 PASS via /app/boost_pricing_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-08). All 17 review-request scenarios verified end-to-end (optional step 18 webhook signature simulation skipped per spec). Endpoints under test live at server.py L7810-7941 + L7481-7575.\n\n  ✅ (1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only) GET /boosts/pricing → 200 with 3 default boosts {triple_day, double_week, double_month} all is_free=true purchased=false; currencies array length=10 (≥10 satisfied).\n  ✅ (2) Non-admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day → 403 'Creator only.' exact match.\n  ✅ (3) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 pricing.price=2.99 is_free=false currency='USD'.\n  ✅ (4) Invalid boost_id 'foo' → 400. (5) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (6) Negative price → 400.\n  ✅ (7) Discount 50% / 7 days → discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price=1.5 (within ±0.01 of expected 1.495 — backend rounds to 2 decimals).\n  ✅ (8) percent=0 clears (discount_active=false, effective_price=2.99). (9) percent=120 → 400. (10) Non-admin discount → 403.\n  ✅ (11) Fresh user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with kind='boost' app_id='triple_day' amount=299 currency='USD' client_secret starts 'pi_test_' customer_id starts 'cus_' publishable_key starts 'pk_test_'.\n  ✅ (12) PI {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  ✅ (13) Admin sets triple_day price=0 → PI {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free — no checkout needed.'\n  ✅ (14) Restore 2.99. Fresh user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} → 200 saved=true is_free=false; GET /profile boost_inventory contains triple_day entry.\n  ✅ (15) Same C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} (still free) → 200 saved=false is_free=true; profile.boost_inventory still ONLY has triple_day (no double_week added — free purchase short-circuits before granting).\n  ✅ (16) POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  ✅ (17) Regression — all prior endpoints 200 OK on normal paths: GET /library/pricing, GET /library/ratings, GET /admin/reports, POST /payments/create-checkout (library), POST /payments/create-payment-intent (default kind=library).\n\n  Webhook step 18 SKIPPED per spec (optional / signature simulation hard). Code-review confirms server.py L7686-7711: payment_intent.succeeded handler with metadata.kind='boost' uses _make_inventory_entry + dedupes on boost_inventory.stripe_payment_intent before $push (idempotent on resend).\n\n  Marked task 'Boost pricing endpoints + payment-intent kind=boost' working:true, needs_retesting:false. No bugs found — main agent can summarise & ship."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Boost Pricing & Purchases (Points+ Boosts shop). Mirrors Library+ pricing endpoints but for the 3 XP boosts (triple_day, double_week, double_month) shown in the Bonus Top Up tab. New endpoints (server.py):\n  • GET /api/boosts/pricing → returns pricing for all 3 boosts + currencies array (same shape as /library/pricing).\n  • POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id} body={price, currency, purchase_url} (Creator-only).\n  • POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}/discount body={percent, duration_value, duration_unit} (Creator-only).\n  • POST /api/boosts/purchase body={boost_id} → trust-based fallback that grants the boost into profile.boost_inventory if priced (free → no-op).\n  • POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent now accepts {kind:'boost', app_id:<boost_id>} → creates a Stripe PaymentIntent with metadata.kind='boost' and metadata.boost_id=<id>. Webhook payment_intent.succeeded handler now branches: kind='boost' → grants boost into inventory idempotently (dedupe on stripe_payment_intent inside boost_inventory); kind='library' → existing library_purchases flow.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Anonymous GET /api/boosts/pricing → 200 with 3 boosts (all default is_free=true, purchased=false), currencies array len ≥10.\n2. Non-admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99} → 403 'Creator only.'\n3. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 with returned pricing.price=2.99, is_free=false.\n4. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/foo {price:1} → 400.\n5. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:5, currency:'XYZ'} → 400.\n6. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 discount_active=true, effective_price≈1.495.\n7. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:0} → clears.\n8. Non-admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount → 403.\n9. As fresh user A, POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200; response has kind='boost', app_id='triple_day', amount=299 (5% off if discount), payment_intent metadata should contain kind='boost' boost_id='triple_day'. (This reads price 2.99 USD set in step 3.)\n10. POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n11. Set triple_day to free → POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free'.\n12. Restore triple_day price 2.99. Webhook test: POST /api/payments/webhook with valid stripe-signature header for event payment_intent.succeeded with metadata={kind:'boost', boost_id:'triple_day', user_id:<B>}. Verify boost is added to user B's profile.boost_inventory (verify via GET /api/profile boost_inventory non-empty). Re-send same webhook → no duplicate (idempotency on stripe_payment_intent field inside the inventory entry).\n13. As fresh user C: POST /api/boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} (trust fallback) → 200 saved=true; profile.boost_inventory now contains the boost.\n14. POST /api/boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} when double_week is free → {saved:false, is_free:true} — no inventory change.\n15. Regression: existing /library/pricing, /library/ratings, /admin/reports, /payments/create-checkout, /payments/create-payment-intent (kind defaults to 'library') all still work.\n\nMark task working:true if all assertions pass."

  - task: "Boost pricing endpoints + payment-intent kind=boost"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Implemented at server.py — added BOOST_IDS = ['triple_day','double_week','double_month'] + BOOST_PRETTY constants. New endpoints: GET /boosts/pricing, POST /boosts/pricing/{id}, POST /boosts/pricing/{id}/discount, POST /boosts/purchase. Reuses _pricing_doc_to_pub helper from library pricing. The existing POST /payments/create-payment-intent now branches on body.kind ('library' default | 'boost'): for boost kind reads price from db.boost_pricing, for library kind reads from db.library_pricing. PaymentIntent metadata includes both kind and boost_id (or app_id) so the webhook can route the unlock correctly. Webhook payment_intent.succeeded handler now grants boost into profile.boost_inventory idempotently (dedupe by stripe_payment_intent field) when metadata.kind='boost'. Frontend: PointsPlusModal renders <PricingBadge> at the bottom-left of each boost card with isAdmin=Creator-tap-to-edit; non-admin tap on a priced boost opens BuyAppModal (kind='boost'); free boosts use the legacy unlock-code+claim flow."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 63/63 assertions in /app/boost_pricing_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). All 17 review-request scenarios verified end-to-end (the optional step 18 webhook signature simulation was skipped per spec). Endpoints under test: GET /api/boosts/pricing, POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}, POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}/discount, POST /api/boosts/purchase, POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent (kind='boost'), all live at server.py L7810-7941 + L7481-7575.\n\n  ✅ (1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only, no Bearer) GET /boosts/pricing → 200 with response.pricing containing exactly the 3 boost_ids {triple_day, double_week, double_month}. Each entry has is_free=true and purchased=false (default state with no doc). response.currencies = ['USD','EUR','GBP','AUD','CAD','JPY','INR','RON','CHF','BRL'] (length=10, ≥10 satisfied).\n\n  ✅ (2) Non-admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 403 with detail='Creator only.' (exact string).\n\n  ✅ (3) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 with body.pricing={price:2.99, currency:'USD', is_free:false, effective_price:2.99}.\n\n  ✅ (4) Invalid boost_id 'foo' → 400 'Invalid boost_id. Must be one of [...]'. (5) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (6) Negative price -1 → 400.\n\n  ✅ (7) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price=1.5 (within tolerance ±0.01 of 1.495 — backend rounds via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` at server.py L7239 so 2.99*0.5=1.495 → 1.5).\n\n  ✅ (8) Admin POST /discount {percent:0} → 200 with discount_active=false, effective_price=2.99 (full price restored).\n\n  ✅ (9) percent=120 → 400 'percent must be 0..99'. (10) Non-admin POST /discount {percent:10, duration_value:1, duration_unit:'days'} → 403.\n\n  ✅ (11) Fresh user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with response.kind='boost', app_id='triple_day', amount=299 (Stripe minor units for $2.99 USD), currency='USD'. payment_intent_client_secret starts with 'pi_test_' (live Stripe test key in use), customer_id starts with 'cus_', publishable_key starts with 'pk_test_'. PaymentIntent metadata correctly carries kind='boost' and boost_id='triple_day' (verified via server.py L7546-7553).\n\n  ✅ (12) POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400 'Invalid boost_id. Must be one of [...]'.\n\n  ✅ (13) Admin sets triple_day to price=0 → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free — no checkout needed.' (substring 'free' satisfies spec).\n\n  ✅ (14) Restore triple_day price=2.99. Fresh user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with saved=true, is_free=false. GET /profile as C → boost_inventory contains a triple_day entry (type='triple_day' from _make_inventory_entry helper at server.py L3289 + _grant_boost_to_inventory at L7908).\n\n  ✅ (15) Same user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} (still free, no doc) → 200 with saved=false, is_free=true. Subsequent GET /profile boost_inventory still has only the triple_day entry — NO double_week added (free-purchase short-circuits at server.py L7937-7938 without granting).\n\n  ✅ (16) POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'foo'} → 400 invalid boost_id.\n\n  ✅ (17) Regression — all prior endpoints 200 OK on normal paths: GET /library/pricing (auth), GET /library/ratings (auth), GET /admin/reports (admin), POST /payments/create-checkout (library mode, fresh user with sleep priced 9.99), POST /payments/create-payment-intent (default kind=library) all returned 200.\n\n  ⚠️ Step 18 (webhook with valid Stripe signature) was SKIPPED per spec ('Optional / skip if hard'). The webhook handler logic itself is verified by code-review at server.py L7686-7711: payment_intent.succeeded event with metadata.kind='boost' calls _make_inventory_entry + dedupes on boost_inventory.stripe_payment_intent before $push — idempotent on resend. Live Stripe-CLI signature simulation requires whsec generation outside the test runner; main agent can validate this with Stripe Dashboard webhook test events if needed.\n\n  No bugs found. Marked task working:true, needs_retesting:false."


      message: "NEW: Stripe PaymentSheet support added for in-app native checkout. New endpoint POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id} returns {payment_intent_client_secret, ephemeral_key_secret, customer_id, publishable_key, amount, currency, effective_price, app_id, payment_intent_id} — used by mobile PaymentSheet. The webhook /api/payments/webhook now ALSO handles event type 'payment_intent.succeeded' (in addition to checkout.session.completed) and inserts a library_purchases row idempotently using metadata.user_id + metadata.app_id. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is now configured (whsec_…). Backend behaviour to verify:\n\n  1. Admin sets sleep price to 9.99 USD (POST /api/library/pricing/sleep).\n  2. Fresh user A POSTs /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with non-empty payment_intent_client_secret starting with 'pi_test_…_secret_', ephemeral_key_secret starting with 'ek_test_', customer_id starting with 'cus_', publishable_key starting with 'pk_test_', amount=999, currency='USD', effective_price=9.99, payment_intent_id starting with 'pi_test_'.\n  3. Re-call as same user A → also 200; should re-use the same Stripe customer (users.stripe_customer_id is set). New PaymentIntent each time but same customer.\n  4. /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  5. Set sleep to free → /api/payments/create-payment-intent → 400 'This app is free'.\n  6. Restore sleep 9.99. Mark user A as already-owning sleep (POST /library/purchase/sleep) → then /payments/create-payment-intent → 409 'You already own this mini-app.'\n  7. Webhook 'payment_intent.succeeded' — register fresh user B. Send POST /api/payments/webhook (no signature header — will fail signature check now since whsec is configured; should return 401). Then send WITH proper Stripe signature: simulate properly-signed event using stripe.WebhookSignature or use Stripe CLI; OR (simpler) temporarily test by removing whsec from env (NOT WANTED). Instead please verify the handler logic with raw payload directly via mongo: insert library_purchases via /library/purchase fallback for the sake of regression, then send webhook with valid sig if possible.\n     Easier alternative: send POST /api/payments/webhook with body={'type':'payment_intent.succeeded','data':{'object':{'id':'pi_test_dummy_b','amount_received':999,'amount':999,'currency':'usd','metadata':{'user_id':'<B>','app_id':'sleep'}}}} — expect 401 (signature missing). Confirm the 401 response.\n  8. Regression — all prior endpoints still PASS (admin pricing CRUD, /payments/create-checkout, /library/ratings, /admin/reports).\n\nMark task working:true if all pass. Stripe SDK version in use: stripe-15.1.0."

Returns everything PaymentSheet's initPaymentSheet() needs. Webhook handler extended to cover 'payment_intent.succeeded' (event types now: checkout.session.completed, payment_intent.succeeded). Signature verification active because STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_… is configured — un-signed payloads now return 401 instead of being accepted.           comment: "PASS — 36/36 assertions in /app/stripe_pi_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). All 9 review-plan steps green:\n\n  ✅ [S1] Admin login + POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200 with pricing.price=9.99, currency='USD', is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [S2] Fresh gmail.com user A (Maya Patel) → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200. Real Stripe call hit. Response shape verified:\n    • payment_intent_client_secret='pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg_secret_fJErOBzqFEHPsesgiP7mH8Hyk' — starts with 'pi_' (NOTE: actual prefix is 'pi_3' for Stripe test mode, NOT 'pi_test_' as the spec states; spec wording is inaccurate — Stripe test-mode IDs use the live-mode prefix 'pi_3...' with the test key. The '_secret_' substring IS present, which is the meaningful assertion).\n    • ephemeral_key_secret starts with 'ek_test_' ('ek_test_YWNjdF8xVFVx…').\n    • customer_id starts with 'cus_' ('cus_UTqYTNnijfr5eN').\n    • publishable_key starts with 'pk_test_' ('pk_test_51TUqjxJriEuN9Pqi…').\n    • amount=999 (exact), currency='USD', effective_price=9.99.\n    • payment_intent_id='pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg' starts with 'pi_' (same NOTE as client_secret — test-mode prefix is 'pi_3' not 'pi_test_').\n\n  ✅ [S3] Re-call POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} as same user A → 200. Body: same customer_id ('cus_UTqYTNnijfr5eN' both calls — verifies users.stripe_customer_id caching at server.py L7509-7521 works), DIFFERENT payment_intent_id ('pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg' → 'pi_3TUsrUJriEuN9Pqi0Vp7Ax2G' — PaymentIntent is one-shot, new one created each call, exactly as spec requires).\n\n  ✅ [S4] POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'foo'} → 400 with detail=\"Invalid app_id. Must be one of ['sleep', 'challenges', 'spot', 'confidence']\".\n\n  ✅ [S5] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 (sleep is_free=true). User A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 with detail='This app is free — no checkout needed.'\n\n  ✅ [S6] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} (restore) → 200. User A POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 with saved=true, already_owned=false, is_free=false (legacy fallback row inserted in library_purchases). Then user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 409 with detail='You already own this mini-app.'\n\n  ✅ [S7 — WEBHOOK SIGNATURE NOW ENFORCED] (a) POST /api/payments/webhook with NO `stripe-signature` header and a valid JSON body {type:'payment_intent.succeeded', data.object:{id:'pi_test_dummy_unsigned', amount_received:999, amount:999, currency:'usd', metadata:{user_id:'fake-user', app_id:'sleep'}}} → **401 {detail:'Invalid signature'}** (was 200 before whsec was set). (b) POST same body with bogus 'stripe-signature: t=123,v1=bogus' → **401 {detail:'Invalid signature'}**. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_wzpkmxkPB7xTYkERRN2QYy51vv6MXUW3 is now active in /app/backend/.env and the verify branch at server.py L7641-7646 correctly rejects un-signed payloads via `_stripe_mod.Webhook.construct_event(body, sig, _stripe_whsec)` raising and being caught.\n\n  ✅ [S8] Discount math: admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true. Fresh user C (Aiden Brooks) → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with amount=500 (50% of 999 cents = 499.5 → Python's round() banker-rounds to 500), effective_price=5.0 (within ±0.01 of 4.995). Discount cleared after test.\n\n  ✅ [S9 — REGRESSION] All existing endpoints still work for fresh user D (Ryan Chen):\n    • POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with session_id='cs_test_a1r276bi4mxHqz62bNIx3OyCxsRcrUe8rHM8eawttJiBCxsMgIHtQW0Jno', checkout_url='https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_…'.\n    • GET /library/pricing → 200 with pricing dict.\n    • Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD'} → 200.\n    • GET /library/ratings → 200 with ratings dict.\n    • POST /library/ratings {app_id:'sleep', stars:5} → 200.\n    • GET /admin/reports (as admin) → 200 with reports list.\n\n  CAVEAT (one-line, NOT a backend bug): The spec asserted 'payment_intent_client_secret starts with pi_test_' and 'payment_intent_id starts with pi_test_'. Stripe's actual test-mode IDs use the prefix 'pi_3' (e.g. 'pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg'), NOT 'pi_test_'. This is correct Stripe behaviour — the 'pi_test_' prefix doesn't exist in modern Stripe API. The meaningful structural assertions (starts with 'pi_' and contains '_secret_' for client_secret) DO hold. Test relaxed accordingly. No code change needed.\n\n  Marked working:true; webhook signature verification is enforced; PaymentSheet endpoint contract honoured exactly."


      message: "NEW: Real Stripe Checkout integration. Test stripe-15.1.0 SDK is installed and STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is configured (test mode sk_test_…). STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is intentionally NOT yet configured (the user has not registered the webhook in their Stripe dashboard yet) — please verify the backend correctly accepts un-verified payloads in that case (logs a warning) and rejects with 401 once a whsec is configured. Endpoints (server.py around L7400):\n  • POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id} → looks up server-side pricing, creates a real Stripe Checkout Session, returns {session_id, checkout_url, amount, currency, effective_price, app_id}. Errors: 400 if app_id invalid / app is free / effective_price=0; 409 if user already owns; 503 if stripe key missing.\n  • POST /api/payments/webhook → on `checkout.session.completed` with payment_status='paid' inserts library_purchases row idempotently using metadata.user_id + metadata.app_id. Idempotent: same session_id → no duplicate insert. Returns {received:true, type}.\n  • GET /api/payments/session/{id}/verify → fallback for when webhook hasn't arrived; queries Stripe directly (will fail in pure unit tests because session_id cs_test_… isn't a real Stripe session — please test with mocked sessions or live Stripe test mode by hitting create-checkout first).\n  • GET /api/payments/return?status=&session_id= → returns HTML that auto-redirects to xpconfidence://payments/return deep link.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Admin login → POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200.\n2. Fresh user A → POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with session_id starting with 'cs_test_', checkout_url starting with 'https://checkout.stripe.com/', amount=999, currency='USD', effective_price=9.99.\n3. POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n4. Set sleep back to free (price:0). POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 'This app is free…'.\n5. Restore sleep price=9.99. POST as user A again → 200. Then mark user A as already-owning sleep (use existing /api/library/purchase/sleep). POST create-checkout again → 409 'You already own this mini-app.'\n6. Webhook idempotency: simulate POST /api/payments/webhook with body={'type':'checkout.session.completed','data':{'object':{'id':'cs_test_dummy_xyz','payment_status':'paid','status':'complete','amount_total':999,'currency':'usd','payment_intent':'pi_test_xyz','metadata':{'user_id':<userB_id>,'app_id':'sleep'}}}} (no signature header — backend should accept since whsec is empty, but log warning). First call inserts library_purchases. Second identical call must NOT insert again (no duplicate row for that user_id/app_id pair).\n7. After webhook (step 6), GET /api/library/pricing as user B → sleep.purchased=true.\n8. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'}. Then user C → POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → amount must be 500 (50% off 999 cents = 499.5 → rounded), effective_price 4.995 or 5.0 (tolerance ±0.01).\n9. GET /api/payments/return?status=success&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 HTML containing 'xpconfidence://payments/return' and the session_id.\n10. GET /api/payments/return?status=cancel&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 HTML with 'cancelled' wording.\n11. Sanity: existing /library/pricing, /library/ratings, /admin/reports endpoints still work.\n\nNote: skip step 8's edge case if Stripe rejects unit_amount=499 due to minimum currency thresholds — in that case just verify amount<999.\nMark task working:true if all assertions pass."

Frontend BuyAppModal updated to call /payments/create-checkout, show a premium 'Redirecting to secure checkout…' overlay (spinner + brand + Stripe shield), then Linking.openURL(checkout_url). Frontend StripeReturnHandler subscribes to deep links and polls /payments/session/{id}/verify on return so OWNED state finalises even if webhook hasn't been registered yet."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-TEST PASS (2026-05-08, /app/stripe_webhook_retest.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api) — 27/27 assertions PASS. Main agent's `import json` fix at server.py L7 resolved the previously broken POST /payments/webhook flow. Step 6 (the only step that previously failed) is now fully green:\n\n  ✅ [6a] Admin login + POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200 with pricing.price=9.99, currency='USD', is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [6b] Registered fresh gmail.com user B (Ryan Chen, ryan.chen.webhook+<rand>@gmail.com); JWT immediate; user_id=8ee73e5f-95f4-4e52-89fe-b21ddf234fb7. Initial GET /library/pricing as B → sleep.purchased=false.\n\n  ✅ [6c] POST /api/payments/webhook (no `stripe-signature` header) with body {type:'checkout.session.completed', data.object:{id:'cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f', payment_status:'paid', status:'complete', amount_total:999, currency:'usd', payment_intent:'pi_test_dummy_b', metadata:{user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'}}} → **200 {received:true, type:'checkout.session.completed'}**. Backend log shows '[stripe] webhook received without whsec verification (set STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)' (correct DEV-only warning) followed by '[stripe] purchase recorded user=8ee73e5f-... app=sleep session=cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f'. The previous `Invalid JSON` error is gone — `json` is now imported at module scope (server.py L7).\n\n  ✅ [6d] GET /api/library/pricing as B → 200 with sleep.purchased=true. Direct DB read via Motor confirmed exactly 1 row in db.library_purchases for {user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'} with paid_amount=9.99, paid_currency='USD', stripe_session_id='cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f', stripe_payment_intent='pi_test_dummy_b', source='stripe', purchased_at ISO timestamp, _id is uuid (not ObjectId).\n\n  ✅ [6e] Re-POST the EXACT same payload → 200 same body. db.library_purchases row count for (B, sleep) STILL == 1 (no duplicate insert). Verified via Motor: `count_documents({user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'}) == 1` after the duplicate POST.\n\n  ✅ [6e-bonus] Varied the session id to a new 'cs_test_dummy_b_<rand2>' but kept SAME user_id+app_id pair → 200; library_purchases count STILL == 1 (the $or check at server.py L7439-7442 catches the (user_id, app_id) pair and skips the insert even when stripe_session_id differs). The original row's stripe_session_id was NOT overwritten because the existing row already has a non-empty session_id (only empty session_ids get backfilled per L7445). sleep.purchased remains true throughout.\n\n  Webhook idempotency works exactly per spec — single library_purchases row per (user_id, app_id) regardless of how many duplicate webhook deliveries Stripe sends. The composite-key uniqueness is enforced via the existing-record $or query (no DB unique index needed). Marked task working:true, stuck_count:0 (cleared), needs_retesting:false."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 37/41 assertions in /app/stripe_checkout_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). 10 of 11 review-plan steps green; STEP 6 (webhook) FAILS because of one CRITICAL backend bug.\n\n  ✅ [S1] Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200; returned pricing.price=9.99, is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [S2] Fresh gmail.com user A (Maya Patel) → POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200. Real Stripe call hit — backend logs show 'Request to Stripe api method=post url=https://api.stripe.com/v1/checkout/sessions response_code=200'. Response body has session_id='cs_test_a1JahmZzpYzkhXw1eFlAPQzWb6hDOoJL2dk1zlUg7YfiuNoUyu4mv777by' (starts cs_test_), checkout_url='https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_…' (starts https://checkout.stripe.com/), amount=999 (exact), currency='USD', effective_price=9.99, app_id='sleep'.\n\n  ✅ [S3] POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'foo'} → 400 with detail=\"Invalid app_id. Must be one of ['sleep', 'challenges', 'spot', 'confidence']\".\n\n  ✅ [S4] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 (sleep is_free=true). User A POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 with detail='This app is free — no checkout needed.'\n\n  ✅ [S5] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 (restore). User A POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 with saved=true (legacy fallback row inserted in library_purchases). Then user A POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 409 with detail='You already own this mini-app.'\n\n  ❌❌❌ [S6 — CRITICAL BUG, BLOCKS PURCHASE FINALISATION] Webhook idempotency BROKEN. Registered fresh user B (Ryan Chen, gmail.com), captured B's user_id from /auth/register (8ab63bab-e0f5-4ea9-bded-584513dd232e). Confirmed B's GET /library/pricing shows sleep.purchased=false. POSTed to /payments/webhook with NO `stripe-signature` header and a perfectly valid JSON body (verified with `python -c 'import json; print(json.loads(body))'` locally). Expected: 200 {received:true, type:'checkout.session.completed'}. Got: **400 {detail:'Invalid JSON'}**. Re-POST: same 400. Direct DB count via Motor confirmed library_purchases for {user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'} stayed at 0 — B's pricing.purchased remained false.\n\n  Verified directly with curl from outside the test runner against the public ingress: `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{...}' https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/payments/webhook` → HTTP/2 400 `{\"detail\":\"Invalid JSON\"}`.\n\n  ROOT CAUSE (server.py L7559): the un-verified-payload branch does `event = json.loads(body.decode(\"utf-8\") or \"{}\")` BUT the `json` module is NOT imported at module scope in server.py. `grep -n '^import json\\|^from json' /app/backend/server.py` returns NOTHING — the only `import json` in the entire file is a LOCAL import on L2123 inside a different function (won't leak out of that scope). So at request-time, `json` is `NameError: name 'json' is not defined`, which the broad `except Exception:` block catches and re-raises as `HTTPException(400, \"Invalid JSON\")`. The error message is misleading — it's not the JSON that's invalid, it's the missing import.\n\n  ONE-LINE FIX: add `import json` to the top of server.py (or inside the webhook handler). After the fix, re-run /app/stripe_checkout_test.py to verify webhook idempotency (single library_purchases row even after duplicate webhook POSTs).\n\n  ✅ [S7] Discount math: admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'} → 200. Fresh user C (Aiden Brooks) → POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with amount=500 (in spec band 499..500), effective_price=5.0 (in spec band 4.99..5.00). Server rounds 9.99*0.5=4.995 → 5.0 via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` then 5.0*100=500 cents — correct.\n\n  ✅ [S8] GET /payments/return?status=success&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 with content-type 'text/html; charset=utf-8'; body contains 'xpconfidence://payments/return', 'cs_test_xyz', AND 'Payment received' (the success H1).\n\n  ✅ [S9] GET /payments/return?status=cancel&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200; body contains 'cancelled' (in 'Payment cancelled' H1 and 'No charge was made' subtext).\n\n  ✅ [S10] GET /payments/session/cs_test_does_not_exist_xyz/verify → 404 with detail='Session not found: Request req_… No such checkout.session: cs_test_does_not_exist_xyz' (real Stripe API was hit and returned 404 resource_missing — confirmed via backend logs).\n\n  ✅ [S11] Regression: GET /library/pricing (user JWT) → 200; GET /library/ratings (user JWT) → 200; GET /admin/reports (admin JWT) → 200. No regressions on the existing surfaces.\n\n  Marked working:false because S6 webhook is non-functional → real Stripe purchases CANNOT be finalised via the webhook path. The /payments/session/{id}/verify fallback DOES work (S10 hit Stripe and returned 404 correctly), so a paying user could still get unlocked via the frontend deep-link verify path, but the server-side webhook is broken until `import json` is added."


      message: "NEW BACKEND BEHAVIOUR to verify: Library+ Mini-App Pricing & Purchases. Endpoints: (1) GET /api/library/pricing → returns pricing for all 4 mini-apps (sleep, challenges, spot, confidence) with per-user `purchased` flag and a list of supported `currencies`. Default state: every app is FREE (price=0, is_free=true, currency=USD, purchase_url=''). (2) POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id} body={price, currency, purchase_url} — Creator-only; sets a price for the app. price=0 makes it free again. Currency must be in SUPPORTED_PRICE_CURRENCIES (USD/EUR/GBP/AUD/CAD/JPY/INR/RON/CHF/BRL). purchase_url must start with http(s):// when non-empty. (3) POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/discount body={percent, duration_value, duration_unit} — Creator-only; sets a time-limited discount. percent=0 clears. duration_unit ∈ days|weeks|months. discount_active=true only while now ∈ [starts_at, ends_at]. effective_price = price * (1 - percent/100) when discount_active. (4) POST /api/library/purchase/{app_id} — records the caller's purchase (idempotent; trust-based after external Ko-fi/Stripe checkout). Free apps return {already_owned:true, is_free:true} no-op. Paid apps insert into library_purchases. Subsequent GET /library/pricing returns purchased=true.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Anonymous GET /api/library/pricing — should return 4 apps all is_free=true, purchased=false, currencies array length 10.\n2. Non-admin user POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} → 403 'Creator only.'\n3. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example'} → 200, returned pricing has price=9.99, currency=USD, is_free=false, effective_price=9.99.\n4. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:5, currency:'XYZ'} → 400.\n5. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:5, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'ftp://bad'} → 400.\n6. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:-1} → 400.\n7. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/foo (invalid app_id) → 400.\n8. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200; discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price≈4.995≈5.0 (round to 2dp). discount_ends_at ~ 7d from now.\n9. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:0} → clears (discount_active=false, effective_price=9.99).\n10. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:50, duration_value:0} → 400.\n11. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:120} → 400.\n12. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:30, duration_value:1, duration_unit:'years'} → 400 (only days/weeks/months).\n13. Non-admin POST /.../discount → 403.\n14. New user A: GET /api/library/pricing → sleep.purchased=false. POST /api/library/purchase/sleep → 200 {saved:true, already_owned:false, is_free:false}. Re-POST → {already_owned:true}. GET /api/library/pricing → sleep.purchased=true.\n15. POST /api/library/purchase on a still-FREE app (e.g. challenges with price=0) → {saved:false, already_owned:true, is_free:true} no-op (no row inserted).\n16. POST /api/library/purchase/foo → 400.\n17. Anonymous user (X-Anonymous-Id) full purchase flow should also work (trust-based per-anon-id).\n18. Admin sets price back to 0 on sleep (price:0) → is_free becomes true again BUT existing user A's purchased flag should still reflect the historical record (purchased=true). [Acceptable either way — please report observed behaviour.]\n19. Regression: existing /library/ratings still works."

Added 4 endpoints (GET/POST /library/pricing[/{id}[/discount]], POST /library/purchase/{id}), 2 collections (library_pricing, library_purchases), helper _pricing_doc_to_pub computing effective_price and discount_active. (5) Ratings 1..5 with text='' (empty string) should still create a feedback row with excerpt='(no comment)'. (6) GET /api/feedback/me returns submitted=true after a successful POST. (8) Regression: existing /admin/reports flows still work — abuse reports (kind='message_text', severity='severe') created via /messages/send still appear; /admin/reports/{id}/view still flips viewed_at; /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss still removes from inbox. POST /api/feedback {rating:5, text:'Love this app, please add dark theme', level_at_submit:3, platform:'ios'} → 200 with body={saved:true, id:'<uuid>'}.\n\n  ✅ [Step 2] Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200 token (len 237). Admin GET /admin/reports → 200 with {reports:list, new_count:int}.\n\n  ✅ [Step 3] Located the row for user A in reports[]. Field-by-field validation: kind=='feedback', severity=='info', rating==5, stars=='★★★★★', excerpt=='Love this app, please add dark theme' (exact match), reason starts with 'In-app feedback (5/5 stars)' (full string was 'In-app feedback (5/5 stars) · Level 3'), level_at_submit==3, platform=='ios', viewed_at is None, reported_email=='maya.patel+...@gmail.com' (matches A.email). Row keys observed: ['created_at','dismissed_at','excerpt','id','kind','level_at_submit','platform','rating','reason','reported_email','reported_name','reported_user_id','severity','stars','viewed_at'] — all required fields present.\n\n  ✅ [Step 4] new_count >= 1 (got 1) and the user-A row counts toward it (viewed_at is None).\n\n  ✅ [Step 5] Edge-case validation: {rating:0}→400 'rating must be between 1 and 5'; {rating:6}→400 same; {text:'abc'} (missing rating)→400 same. After EACH failed POST, a re-fetch of /admin/reports confirmed the feedback-row count for A stayed at the baseline (1) — no leaks. The handler raises HTTPException BEFORE the admin_reports insert, so failed validation never produces phantom notifications.\n\n  ✅ [Step 6] Empty text: POST /api/feedback {rating:3, text:''} → 200. Newest row for A has rating==3, excerpt=='(no comment)' (exact), stars=='★★★☆☆' (3 filled, 2 empty). Confirms the `excerpt = text if text else '(no comment)'` fallback at server.py L6989 and the stars formatter `'★'*n + '☆'*(5-n)`.\n\n  ✅ [Step 7] GET /api/feedback/me as A → 200 with submitted=True and submitted_at='2026-05-08T14:02:38.352216+00:00' (ISO 8601, parseable).\n\n  ✅ [Step 8] 2nd submission: POST /api/feedback {rating:4, text:'Second piece of feedback'} → 200. /admin/reports now has ≥3 feedback rows for A with ratings {3,4,5} all present — no dedupe enforced (each POST inserts a fresh admin_reports row, as designed).\n\n  ✅ [Step 9] Anonymous flow: POST /api/feedback with header X-Anonymous-Id=anon-feedback-<uuid> (no Bearer) {rating:2, text:'Anonymous user feedback'} → 200. /admin/reports now contains a row with reported_email=='', reported_name=='Anonymous', rating=2, excerpt='Anonymous user feedback'. reported_user_id observed as 'anon-anon-feedback-<uuid>' (the get_user_or_legacy convention: 'anon-' prefix + the X-Anonymous-Id value).\n\n  ✅ [Step 10] Regression /admin/reports flow: non-admin GET → 403 with detail 'Admin only.'; POST /admin/reports/{id}/view by admin → 200 {ok:true}; subsequent GET shows that row's viewed_at populated as ISO timestamp ('2026-05-08T14:02:39.902125+00:00') AND new_count decremented exactly by 1 (4→3). POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss by admin → 200 {ok:true}; subsequent GET no longer contains that row (filter dismissed_at:null at server.py L6283 enforced).\n\n  ✅ [Step 11] Sanity smoke (unchanged endpoints): /profile (JWT) → 200; /stats/weekly → 200; /library/ratings → 200.\n\n  No bugs found. The admin_reports notification side-effect on POST /api/feedback works exactly per spec and is correctly skipped on validation failures (try/except wrap in the notify block does NOT swallow errors that originate before the insert — the HTTPException for invalid rating is raised earlier). Marked task working:true, needs_retesting:false. Main agent can summarise & ship."

      message: "Library+ Mini-App Ratings — 66/66 PASS via /app/library_ratings_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-08).\n\n  ✅ GET /api/library/ratings: response shape {ratings:{sleep:{...}, challenges:{...}, spot:{...}, confidence:{...}}}; each app stat has exactly 3 keys {average,count,user_rating}; user_rating=null for fresh user across all 4 apps. NOTE: aggregate count is GLOBAL across all users by design (server.py L6823 `find({})` with no user filter). On this live DB sleep already had count=2 from prior test runs; user_rating=null for the fresh user is the per-user assertion that matters and IS satisfied. The 'fresh user gets count=0 everywhere' part of the spec only holds in a clean DB; the implementation is functionally correct (ratings.x.average is a community average shown to everyone).\n\n  ✅ POST /api/library/ratings:\n    A (create sleep=4): 200 saved=true, app_id='sleep', stats.user_rating=4.\n    B (update sleep 4→5): 200, user_rating=5, count UNCHANGED — confirms upsert (composite key user_id+app_id), NOT insert. Follow-up GET confirms count identical.\n    C (different app challenges=3): 200, user_rating=3; GET shows sleep.user_rating still 5, spot/confidence still null.\n    D (multi-user aggregation): registered second gmail.com user; their initial sleep.user_rating=null (cross-user isolation works); they POST {sleep,1} → 200, sleep.count incremented by exactly 1 (2→3 in this run); user1 GET shows sleep.count=3, user1.user_rating still 5 (their own, NOT affected by user2). Average correctly recomputed (3.33 here with 3 raters; would be exactly 3.0 in a clean DB with only the two test users).\n    E (validation): all 5 invalid bodies returned 400 — stars=0, stars=6, stars='abc', invalid app_id, missing stars. Handler uses raw Body(...) dict and validates manually, so it returns 400 (not Pydantic 422).\n    F (anonymous via X-Anonymous-Id): set header, no Authorization → GET 200 (all user_rating=null), POST {sleep,2} → 200 saved, follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating=2 persisted under that anon id, AND a different X-Anonymous-Id sees null (correct per-anon-id isolation).\n\n  ✅ Regression smoke (fresh JWT user): /profile→200, /stats/weekly→200 days=7, /stats/monthly→200 days=30, /stats/by-area→200 with all 4 areas, /library/catalog as admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com)→200, /library/catalog as non-admin→403.\n\n  ✅ MongoDB collection `library_ratings` and document shape {user_id, app_id, stars, updated_at, _id} verified indirectly via correctness — Scenario B's count-unchanged proves composite (user_id, app_id) uniqueness via upsert; Scenario D proves global aggregation; Scenario F proves anonymous user_id derivation works.\n\n  No bugs found. NEW BACKEND in server.py: 9 endpoints — POST /messages/refine (real-time GPT-4o-mini grammar/safety scrub, returns {refined, flagged, severity, reason}; severe → refined='' and admin reported), POST /messages/check-image (GPT-4o-mini vision check before attaching), POST /messages/send (defence-in-depth re-pass before storing; fires Expo push to recipient via https://exp.host/--/api/v2/push/send; severe content blocks send + files admin report), GET /messages/threads (one row per friend with last-message preview + unread count), GET /messages/thread/{friend_id} (full message list, friend-only), POST /messages/read (mark thread read), GET /messages/unread-summary (per-friend unread counts → drives red dots in Friends list), POST /push/register-token (frontend Expo push token registry, multi-device upsert), GET /admin/reports (Creator-only inbox + new_count for bell badge), POST /admin/reports/{id}/view, POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss. New collections: messages, push_tokens, admin_reports. NEW FRONTEND: /messages/index.tsx (threads list w/ unread badges, polled every 4s), /messages/[friendId].tsx (chat screen with debounced AI refinement card ABOVE the input — user can ONLY send refined text; severe content shows red BLOCKED state and disables Send; image picker pre-checks safety; polls 3s for new messages), /admin/reports.tsx (Creator-only inbox with severity pills + dismiss long-press, golden theme). Friends/index PlayerCard now shows red dot with unread count next to friend name, fed by api.messagesUnreadSummary on each tab focus. Friend profile detail modal: 'friends' status now shows BOTH a Message button (cyan, primary, pushes /messages/{user_id}) and the existing 'Already Friends' chip below it. Profile screen renders an AdminNotificationBell in the top-right ONLY for is_admin profiles — 40px golden circle bell with red badge that polls /api/admin/reports every 8s while focused; tap pushes /admin/reports. After Allow-Notifications grant, NotificationPermissionPrompt now also calls Notifications.getExpoPushTokenAsync() and POSTs the token to /api/push/register-token so the recipient device can be woken even when the app is closed. New _layout.tsx files added for messages/, admin/, spot/multiplayer/ (expo-router needs these for nested routes to resolve). All web routes confirmed loading (admin/reports, messages list both render). NEEDS BACKEND TESTING: full DM flow (refine → send → thread → read), severe-content blocking, admin report creation/view/dismiss, friends-only gate (403 on stranger), unread summary correctness, push token register idempotency. NOTE: Expo Push only delivers to mounted devices that already registered tokens — first useful test on real device after rebuild."

      message: "Tested all 4 newly-added features against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api via /app/backend_test.py. 57/58 assertions PASS, 1 CRITICAL FAIL.\n\n  ✅ Boost Inventory (27/27): unlock with wrong code→400, correct code 'XP270905W20'→200; claim before unlock→403 boosts_locked; claim after unlock→200 returns {claimed, profile} with inventory growing; activate by inventory_id→200 sets active_boost (multiplier=3, expires_at, type); status filters out activated entries; bogus inventory_id→404; legacy {type} activate path still works.\n\n  ✅ Weekly Leaderboard (13/13): GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 for fresh anon user → 1 self row weekly_xp=0; tz param persists to profile.tz_offset_minutes (verified 330); after completing default task (xp=15), self.weekly_xp=15 confirming xp_events doc was inserted; idempotent across same-day calls; reports[] field present; rows sorted desc by weekly_xp (verified on 2-member LB in report test).\n\n  🚨 Report System (8/9 — CRITICAL FAIL): POST /api/leaderboard/report returns 500 on the FIRST successful submit. Root cause: server.py ~line 3037 returns the freshly-inserted Mongo doc which Motor mutates in-place to add a non-JSON-serializable ObjectId `_id`. Backend log shows: `ValueError: [TypeError(\"'ObjectId' object is not iterable\")]`. The DB insert DOES succeed (next duplicate-report request correctly returns 400). Fix: `doc.pop('_id', None)` before `return {'report': doc}` (or build a fresh return dict). Every other report-system flow works: self-report→400, non-LB-member→400, duplicate-same-week→400, support→200 with supporters_count=2, unsupport→200 with supporters_count=1, GET /friends/leaderboard surfaces the report with reporter_name/reported_name/reason/week_key/supporters_count/viewer_is_reporter all populated correctly.\n\n  ✅ Player Profile (8/8): GET /leaderboard/profile/{B_id}?tz=0 → 200 returning standard player payload + weekly_xp:int + medals:[] + is_flagged_cheater:false; bogus uuid → 404.\n\n  Existing tests not affected: anonymous mode, 200-level XP, un-tick, custom XP cap, tasks API, sleep, auth still verified by prior runs."
    - agent: "main"
      message: "Added four new feature areas to backend (all user-scoped + anon-aware): (1) Boost inventory system — profile now carries boost_inventory[]; new POST /api/boosts/claim (adds to inventory after code unlock), POST /api/boosts/activate now accepts {inventory_id} (preferred) or legacy {type}, marks the entry activated=true with activated_at/expires_at; GET /api/boosts/status returns the un-activated inventory list. Added a new 'double_day' boost-def (2x for 1 day) reserved for leaderboard winners. (2) XP-event logging in complete_task — each task completion now writes a document to xp_events with {user_id, xp, earned_at_utc, tz_offset_minutes, local_week_key}. Profile now stores tz_offset_minutes (updatable via PUT /api/profile). (3) Weekly leaderboard — GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=<offset_minutes> returns rows ranked by weekly_xp using EACH player's own local Mon-Sat window (per-user timezone: players in different timezones each run their own 12:00-to-12:00 Sunday boundary). On the viewer's local Sunday, we compute the winner of the preceding Mon-Sat window and idempotently insert a medal + push a 'double_day' 2x-XP boost into the winner's boost_inventory via db.leaderboard_medals. (4) Report system — POST /api/leaderboard/report {reported_user_id, reason}; POST/DELETE /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support; these are stored in db.leaderboard_reports keyed by week_key. If a reported player is about to be declared winner and a strict majority (>= floor(N/2)+1 of leaderboard members including friends + self) have supported the report, their medal is revoked (inserted with revoked=true) and no bonus is granted. (5) New endpoint GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{user_id}?tz=<offset> returns player profile + medals[] + is_flagged_cheater. No existing endpoints were altered in breaking ways (boost/activate still accepts legacy {type} for back-compat). Please test these 5 capabilities + verify existing task/profile/sleep flows still pass."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Tested all 4 newly-added/modified backend features against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api via /app/backend_test.py. 37/38 assertions PASS, 0 critical fails. (1) 200-level XP system: /api/levels returns the full table with max_level=200, total_xp_cap=1000000, formula 'cum_xp(L) = round(49.6 * L^1.87)', L1=0, L50=74569 (in 73000-76000 band), L200=996340 (in 990000-1000000 band). (2) Un-tick: complete + uncomplete round-trip works perfectly — 15 XP awarded then refunded, profile rolls back, task shows completed=false. xp_removed field returned. (3) Custom XP cap=20: POST 150→20, 10→10, 20→20; PUT custom 999→20; PUT default 80→80 (unrestricted); fresh user defaults intact at [15,40,10,30,15,20,10,20]. (4) Anonymous mode: no-header→main, two distinct X-Anonymous-Id values give isolated profiles (anon A xp=20 after task complete, anon B xp=0), too-short ID falls back to main, JWT request ignores the header. The single 'failure' in the test output ('1000 XP -> level 5 per /levels table :: level for 1000 XP = 4') is a SPEC TYPO, not a backend bug — the spec said 'with 1000 XP, level should be 5 (since L5 cum_xp=1006)' but mathematically 1000 < 1006 means level 4, which is what the backend correctly returns. Backend logic is correct. No fixes needed."
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Ran full backend test suite for the updated Tasks API (/app/backend_test.py). All 12 assertions pass against the public ingress URL. If the main agent wants default tasks to persist for end users without requiring a reset, consider a one-time migration that marks existing seeded titles as is_default=true, or change POST /api/seed to upsert the flag on legacy docs. Functionality itself (LOCKED_DEFAULT_FIELDS + delete protection + custom-task full edit/move/delete) is correct."

      message: "Auth + per-user data isolation tested end-to-end (/app/backend_test.py, 26 assertions, 25 PASS / 1 CRITICAL FAIL). All auth flows work (register→dev_code, verify wrong/correct, /auth/me with+without token, login, login wrong pw, resend), per-user XP isolation verified (Carol XP=15 after completing default; Dan XP=0 with disjoint task ids), once-per-day uncomplete returns 400, default-task DELETE returns 400, wake_time PUT/GET roundtrip works, custom-date /tasks?date=2026-04-25 returns 200, sleep onboarding is per-user (Carol onboarded; Dan onboarded:false). 🚨 CRITICAL BUG — the 11-custom-task limit is NOT user-scoped in /app/backend/server.py L746: `db.tasks.count_documents({\"is_default\": {\"$ne\": True}})` is missing `user_id`. As soon as the global custom-task collection has ≥11 docs (already true on this DB), every newly-registered user is blocked from creating a single custom quest. Fix: change to `db.tasks.count_documents({\"user_id\": user_id, \"is_default\": {\"$ne\": True}})`. Test 9b (the 12th-task block) returns the right 400 message, but test 9a (Dan's 1st custom task) was rejected for the wrong reason because the counter is global. After the one-line fix this whole task should pass."
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Day Anchor System regression — 76/76 PASS (/app/backend_test.py, run 2026-04-27 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api).\n\n  ✅ Profile schema: GET /api/profile returns day_start_time / timezone / onboarding_tz_done. Fresh anon profile has all three null/false.\n\n  ✅ Day-anchor write lock: PUT /api/profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} on a fresh profile → 200 with both persisted and onboarding_tz_done=true. Subsequent PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} → 400 detail.error='tz_locked' and value unchanged. PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked profile → 400 detail.error='day_start_locked'. POST /api/profile/reset clears tz/day_start back to null and onboarding_tz_done=false; the original PUT works again afterwards.\n\n  ✅ Tz-aware GET /api/challenge/today: with tz=Australia/Sydney + day_start=07:00 returns 200 with challenge {id, title, ...}.\n\n  ✅ user_today_str propagation in /sleep/checkin: after sleep onboarding, POST /sleep/checkin → 200 with entry.date matching the Sydney-local date (2026-04-28 in this run while UTC date was 2026-04-27 — proving tz-aware computation, not raw server UTC).\n\n  ✅ Challenge past 24h answer window: GET /api/challenge/past returns {completions, count}; freshly-completed entry has can_answer:bool (False, as it was not auto-uncompleted) and answer_deadline field present (None or ISO string). POST /api/challenge/past/{fake-id}/answer → 404.\n\n  ✅ Regression — auth (register/login/me, wrong-pw 401), /api/profile GET, /api/boosts/* (unlock, claim, activate by inventory_id, status), /api/friends/leaderboard (rows/reports/week_key/self row), /api/leaderboard/report (self-report 400), /api/tasks (create custom, complete with body{date}, uncomplete with body{date}, XP rollback verified), /api/goals (create with target_value+unit+xp_reward clamped, list, update, progress tick, delete) — all green.\n\n  Note: Email verification has been disabled in the backend — /api/auth/register now returns a JWT immediately and user.verified=true (no dev_code/verify step). Adjusted backend_test.py accordingly. No critical issues. Marked all four affected task entries working:true and added a new 'Day Anchor System' task entry summarizing items 1-5."

All Spot endpoints + edge cases passed.\n\n  ✅ Profile additions: GET /api/profile fresh anon user → spot_points=0 (int), spot_random_enabled=False (bool). After /spot/complete success=true, spot_points becomes 1.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/object: 8 calls returned 8 distinct curated objects ('anything green', 'cat', 'chair', 'coin', 'flower', 'mug', 'pair of glasses', 'window'); each call returns {object, challenge_id} with challenge_id a fresh uuid; all returned objects are members of SPOT_OBJECTS.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/check: empty photo→400. >8MB b64→400. Positive case (real leaf photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=True, confidence=1.0, can_capture=True. Negative case (real chair photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=False, confidence=0.0, can_capture=False. After 2 completions → 2 entries each with player_name, player_avatar_base64, player_spot_points, liked_by_you, like_count, comment_count, is_self all populated; is_self=True for self-entries.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/like: toggle 0→1→0 with liked_by_you flipping accordingly.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/comment: whitespace-only→400. Normal comment 'Beautiful find! 🌿' → 200 with full doc {id, user_id, user_name, user_avatar_base64, text, created_at}. Bogus uuid → 404.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/random-toggle: enabled=true → spot_random_enabled=True; GET /profile reflects True. enabled=false → flips back to False; GET /profile reflects False.\n\n  ✅ Regression sanity: /api/profile (200), /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (rows/reports/week_key/viewer_is_sunday + self row), /api/tasks complete (xp_awarded=15) + uncomplete (xp_removed=15) — all healthy.\n\n  No bugs found.
